title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Botany by Plant Family: The Key to See
location: Onsite at Duke Gardens – directions to follow
description:
Learning to notice plant family patterns can greatly aid in demystifying the “green wall” of plant species around us. Nearly 350,000 species of flowering plants are known to global science, and these species have been grouped into more than 400 flowering plant families. By learning the top 30 plant families growing around you, you will begin to recognize plants everywhere you go. After an indoor presentation on the major plant families common to the Southeastern U.S., ethnobotanist marc williams will reinforce your plant identification skills on a walk in the Gardens to observe family patterns in leaf, flower and fruit types. Discuss edibility, medicinality, craft, wildlife support and landscape beauty as you gain a more holistic understanding of the major plants comprising Southern U.S. flora and their potential ecological and ethnobotanical applications.
Fee: $32; 20% discount for Duke Gardens members with discount code.
Parking: Fees apply.
Location: Onsite at Duke Gardens. Meeting location will be included in a program reminder email sent before the program.
Accessibility: This will be a small group program that takes place partially seated indoors and partially outdoors traveling on a combination of paved and unpaved paths with some uneven surfaces and moderate inclines.

title: Cultivating Courage by Covering the Corners
location: Corners all over the Triangle and beyond!, Durham, NC, 27705
description:
Join us on the first Sunday of the month as we do a coordinated demonstration on multiple corners (Feb 1 is just for colleges and has its own link). All events are from 1pm-2:30pm as we host “Cultivating Courage by Covering Corners,” a peaceful visibility event designed to build courage and unity in support of democracy.

Please sign up for your corner on SignUp Genius:

Small groups will gather on consecutive corners along a key stretch of road, holding positive, pro-democracy signs. We will cover corners in areas where ED4D does typically do not demonstrate: Creedmoor, Holly Springs, and XXX. All of these areas are purple.

The purpose is to build motivation for folks to join us. By standing together on multiple corners simultaneously, we show our community that courage is contagious—that even small acts of presence can inspire others to stand up for shared values.

Each participant plays an essential role: being visible, being brave, and encouraging others to do the same. The goal is to create a ripple of courage that spreads across our community and beyond.

We will have one common sign at each corner. People will need to bring their own signs. Please also consider bring an American flag along with one of several other flags (rainbow flag, Palestine flag, Mexican flag, Ukraine flag), to make it clear we are not MAGA. We have rainbow flags for each corner.

Please make signs that say, “Stand up, you are not alone!” “Resist!” “Courage is contagious!” "Call your elected officials" "Vote" "We the People Have Power!" "Start your own demonstration!!"

Please join ED4D's Substack: https://substack.com/profile/115484101-engaged-durhamites-4-democracy

For more ED4D Mobilize Events: https://www.mobilize.us/engageddurhamitesfordemocracy/

https://www.mobilize.us/engagedefendersfordemocracy/event/867070/

title: The Art and Science of Plant Care
description:
Caring for plants is both an art and a science — part intuition, part know-how. Every gardener, from beginner to expert, faces the same essential questions: When should I prune? How much should I water? What’s the best way to plant, fertilize or repot? In this class, Bryce Lane will bring decades of horticultural expertise to demystify these everyday tasks, showing you not just what to do but why it works.

Over eight weeks, you’ll explore the fundamental practices that keep gardens thriving, learning how to perform each one with confidence and purpose. You’ll uncover the science behind good technique — understanding how soil, water, nutrients and plant biology interact — while also developing the practical judgment that comes only with experience.

Each session will focus on one key aspect of plant care, building a complete toolkit for growing healthier, more resilient plants in any garden setting.

title: 2026 Wade H. Hargrove Media Law and Policy Colloquium
description:
The Center for Media Law and Policy organizes and hosts the Wade H. Hargrove Media Law and Policy Colloquium. The colloquium was established in 2009 to honor Wade Hargrove, a graduate of the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and the UNC School of Law, and to provide a forum for the discussion of media law and media policy issues. It was funded in part by Hearst Communications, the North Carolina Association of Broadcasters, and the North Carolina Cable Telecommunications Association to recognize the more than 30 years of service that Mr. Hargrove provided to these organizations as well as media and technology companies throughout North Carolina and the nation.

This year's speaker is Kevin Martin. Kevin Martin is the vice president and head of global policy at Meta where he oversees Meta's global work with policymakers on privacy, connectivity, trade, and other issues. Previously, he served as Meta's VP for U.S. Public Policy and chairman (2005-2009) and commissioner (2001-2005) of the Federal Communications Commission. After leaving the FCC, he joined the Aspen Institute as a senior fellow at the think tank's Communications and Society Program, and later the law firm Squire Patton Boggs LLP as a partner. He holds a B.A. from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, an M.P.P. from Duke University, and a J.D. from Harvard Law School.

title: Civic Hacking for Affordable Housing
description:
Code with the Carolinas - Triangle
Join volunteers at Code with the Carolinas as we help the [National Zoning Atlas](https://www.zoningatlas.org/) (NZA) team include North Carolina and South Carolina in their nationwide zoning atlas. "The National Zoning Atlas aims to depict key aspects of zoning codes in an online, user-friendly map." The Atlas aims to provide data for better solutions in transportation, environmental, and especially affordable housing policy.

Affordable housing proponents have long recognized zoning policies as barriers to affordable housing. To improve zoning policies, we first need to better understand the current state of zoning across the United States. Code with the Carolinas is helping this cause by joining the National Zoning Atlas to gather zoning data for North and South Carolina.

This is a multi-dimensional project grounded in data science with an end-goal of contributing to a nationally influential dataset. Our current work session activities focus on internet search and integrating data from multiple sources. The project as a whole has occasional opportunities for U/X and outreach and opportunities to learn about machine learning and natural language processing through the work of the NZA team. Volunteering on this project will provide excellent exposure to the foundations of civic data science, with a focus on GIS data.

Even if you're new to this project and/or civic tech volunteering, you're welcome to join the meeting. Visitors are always welcome and no technical skills are needed. You can learn more about the project on our [Slack Workspace](https://join.slack.com/t/codeforthecarolinas/shared_invite/zt-1kxuwu05x-3KxOpkOYjAuN5yuOAH8ROg) in the #project-zoning-atlas channel.

Meetings start with brief introductions. New volunteers are offered a personal orientation, often in a breakout room, while experienced volunteers get right to work. By the end of the session, both groups of volunteers are working together. We also contribute to the project through asynchronous work and informal work sessions.

Meetings are on the Jitsi platform and the link is provided before each meeting. If you will be joining on mobile, install the Jitsi app in advance [https://jitsi.org/downloads/](https://jitsi.org/downloads/) .

Learn more at [codewiththecarolinas.org](codeforthecarolinas.org) or reach out to info@codewiththecarolinas.org

All activities of Code with the Carolinas, a project of [Social Good Fund](https://www.socialgoodfund.org/), follow our [Code of Conduct](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/0d32d5f905f8fd49d5e80395f8fd47f2b59ce3ef8b335f72/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTkx) and are subject to this [Volunteer Waiver of Liability](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/06dd62200e6b28426b137e0e6b2848fb901487014f72cd16/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTE2).

description:
Please join us on a monthly basis for a walk through the Lake Raleigh Woods, one of NC State's hidden gems and Hallowed Spaces. The hike is about 1.5 miles. Below are a few things to remember for the hike:

Make sure to park in the northwest corner of the Park Alumni Center parking lot a little before 9 a.m. for a brief safety conversation. Parking is free for everyone. Wear closed-toed shoes and bring a water bottle.Check the weather forecast and wear weather-appropriate clothing on the hike.Restrooms are available in the Alumni Center. There are no facilities on the trail.The hike will last approximately 90 minutes but could be a little shorter depending on the group's pace. Space is limited for each hike. Be sure to register.

title: Project Learning Tree - Together for Birds
description:
Join other K-5 classroom and nonformal educators as we explore Project Learning Tree activities focused on birds.

Together for Birds is PLT’s Newest Activity Collection!

Birds are a wonderful introduction to the natural world and happen to be just outside your door! Whether in the city or a forest, a variety of birds are usually within easy eyeshot or earshot. The activities are designed for educators to use with students in grades K-2, with variations for grades 3-5. The collection is filled with enhanced bird-specific content such as new enrichment experiences, recommended reading, forest facts, accompanying posters, and charts. Every participant will leave with a guide of this collection.

This workshop counts toward NC Environmental Education Certification (Criteria I) and consists of six contact hours that may count toward CEUs for teachers.

Location: Carolina Beach State Park
Workshop Cost: $25
The PLT activity guides are provided free of charge to participants through support from the NC Forestry.
For additional workshop information, call 910-772-0546.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Autonomy: Strengthening Your Ability To Work Independently
description:
If organizations are to continue to be flexible and innovative to meet the challenges of the future, employees will need to not only be responsible for their own actions and decisions but will also need to have the ability to work independently. Join Learning and Organizational Development for a workshop that will focus on the skills necessary to develop autonomy and the characteristics of individuals who demonstrate autonomy. By the end of this workshop, you will:

Understand the difference between accountability and autonomyKnow the characteristics of people who demonstrate autonomyRecognize the impact autonomy has on work satisfactionReflect and evaluat how you make decisions

The first 20 people to sign up for the book club will receive a free book and reading kit curated by the African American Cultural Center Library, Multicultural Students Affairs, the Women’s Center, the LGBTQ Pride Center and the NC State Libraries.

The book club sessions will take place:

Jan. 22 from noon to 2 p.m. in the LGBTQ Pride Center at Talley Student UnionJoin us for a meet and greet and introduction to Song in a Weary Throat.Feb. 5 from noon to 2 p.m. in the African Cultural Center Library at the Witherspoon Student CenterWe will discuss chapters 1-3 and 7 and 8.Feb. 24 from noon to 2 p.m. in Multicultural Student Affairs at Talley Student UnionWe will discuss chapters 9, 11, 15, 16 and 19.March 24 from noon to 2 p.m. in the Women’s Center at Talley Student UnionWe will discuss chapters 20-22, 24-25 and 26. April 7 from noon to 2 p.m. in the Fishbowl at D.H. Hill LibraryWe will discuss chapters 27, 29-30 and 34.

title: 2026 Bobby Boyd Leadership Lecture
description:
The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill School of Social Work is pleased to welcome Maxine Eichner as the guest speaker for the 2026 annual Bobby Boyd Leadership Lecture Series on April 7, 2026.

Eichner’s lecture examines how the United States came to expect so much of families with so little public support, and how this gap has led overwhelming numbers of poor and disproportionately Black families to be trapped in the nation’s child welfare system. She traces how policy has come to emphasize family responsibility without parallel investment in the conditions that make children’s welfare truly possible, while calling to build systems that meet families earlier and to align policy with what children and families need to flourish.

Eichner is the Graham Kenan Distinguished Professor of Law at UNC School of Law. She is both a lawyer and political scientist. She writes on issues of families and inequality; U.S. social welfare law and policy; and the relationship of the family, the workplace, and market forces. She is the author of The Free-Market Family: How the Market Crushed the American Dream (and How It Can Be Restored) (OUP, 2020), and The Supportive State: Families, Government, and America’s Political Ideals (OUP, 2010).

This event will be presented virtually and in person in the auditorium of the Tate-Turner-Kuralt Building on UNC’s campus. The presentation starts at 12:30 p.m. and is free and open to the public. Participants are required to register for the event. A free lunch will be held in the TTK lobby for attendees beginning at 11:30 a.m.

title: Leveraging Generative AI for Research Tasks
description:
Want to know how generative AI (GenAI) can be used to support your research? This workshop covers various GenAI tools that have applications for research tasks including tools that support writing manuscripts, exploring and summarizing literature (e.g., Research Rabbit, Consensus, and Elicit), performing evidence synthesis (including systematic and scoping reviews), and data analysis. An overview of library resources that support GenAI including the Library AI Studio is also provided.

This workshop is offered by the UNC Health Sciences Library and led by Matti Kauftheil.

title: Plant-Lover's Tour: Outrageous Flowering Plants
description: The JC Raulston Arboretum is always beautiful because of its amazing diversity of plants, but things tend to get a bit outrageous when spring has truly sprung. Come go on a guided tour at the arboretum that will highlight the most spectacular, the most vibrant and the most outrageously floriferous plants popping off at the arboretum in early April. With trees, shrubs and herbaceous perennials absolutely dripping in flowers, you'll experience one of the most vibrant moments of the year. You'll leave wowed and with a camera roll full of inspiration.

title: *IN-PERSON* SF/F Group
description:
Durham Writers Group
We will be at the People's Solidarity Hub at 1809 Chapel Hill Rd, Durham. This venue does not serve food or drink, but feel free to bring your own. There is a kitchenette for our use, but be sure to clean up afterwards. The space might be a little cozy; please consider masking or staying home if you have a respiratory ailment.

This sub-group focuses on Speculative Fiction, including Science Fiction, Fantasy and Horror. If you have anything that falls under that category — be it short story, poem, novel-in-progress or screenplay — then come! We'd love to hear from you.

Our format allows each reader 15 minutes to present their work aloud, followed by approximately 20 minutes of feedback from the other attendees. To ensure fairness, each attendee is limited to 1 minute of Initial Feedback time. During that period, the author remains silent. Next, we have a 5 minute Free-For-All discussion about the work -- then the author may speak. (We usually get through three readers.)

Though not required, in order to receive more in-depth feed-back, readers may post their work in advance on our Google Group:
https://groups.google.com/d/forum/durham-sff-writers-group

We ask that participants keep their comments constructive and respectful, focusing on what works or doesn't work in the piece.

We also ask that readers provide a warning, both on the Google Group and during the meeting, if their story features content that some readers might find upsetting. This includes descriptions of violence or sexual content.

Readers are:
•
•
•

Backup Readers are:

Deep Backup:

***We meet every Tuesday at the same time, but alternate between In-Person*** ***and on Zoom. Members may attend both formats, if desired, but readers will be listed separately under the In-Person or Zoom weeks.***

title: Big Tree Paddle
description:
Few people know about the "Big Tree" at Merchants Millpond, and even fewer have actually been to it.

The "Big Tree", as many call it, is actually the second largest tree in the state of North Carolina, and we periodically lead paddles to it. Be warned: this is an all day adventure and not for the faint of heart. This paddle lasts from about 9AM-4PM but it is well worth it! Paddlers should bring appropriate clothing, plenty of water, a packed lunch, and sunscreen. A good hat is also recommended. Depending on water level of the millpond, there is always a chance paddlers will have to drag boats over beaver dams. There is a limited number of spots for this evet and registration prior to the event date is required.

Come out and learn about some of the things that make Merchants Millpond such an interesting place to be!

We will leave at 9:00 AM sharp. Please meet at the visitor center around 8:30 AM for orientation.

To register, please call the visitor center at 252-357-1191

title: Being Adaptive in an Ever-Changing World
description:
“Because we’ve always done it like that,” may not be the best answer to, “Why do we do the things we do?” In our ever-changing world, we are seeing shifts in our markets and economies, our cultural norms, our behaviors, and ourselves. Now is the time to thrive by learning how to be adaptive and to be consistently able to change yourself to accommodate and maximize the benefits of change.

Workshop Objectives
By the end of this workshop, you will:
• Differentiate between being adaptive and similar concepts
• Recognize the importance of being adaptive in work and life
• Examine traits of highly adaptive people
• Outline action steps to take to improve adaptive skills

Click on Carolina Talent registration link and select the “Request” button to automatically enroll.

title: Modular Design and Automated Testing with R
description:
Research is relying on increasingly complex code. However, most academics have not received formal training in software development. This course will introduce participants to some of the best practices of professional software development. Participants will learn to structure their Python code into reusable functions and store those functions as separate scripts and/or packages. Participants will learn to write automated tests for their functions, to help confirm that results are as expected and to ensure that future changes to code do not cause unexpected changes to results.

We will also discuss how to create reproducible “environments” that record package versions in use to help avoid package version incompatibilities and ensure that results can be replicated even months or years after the original analysis.

Modular design is best paired with a version control system. We will discuss how modular design practices integrate with Git and Github, and how to set up “continuous integration” so that automated tests are run each time code is changed.

The course will primarily consist of a hands-on exercise to restructure a single long analysis file into functions and automated tests.

“Version Control with Git and Github” course is a prerequisite for this course.

Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: April 8 - Getting Started with FiftyOne
description:
Raleigh AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision Meetup
This workshop provides a technical foundation for managing large scale computer vision datasets. You will learn to curate, visualize, and evaluate models using the [open source FiftyOne app](https://docs.voxel51.com/).

**Date, Time and Location**

Apr 8, 2026
10 AM PST - 11 AM Pacific
Online. **[Register for the Zoom!](https://voxel51.com/events/getting-started-with-fiftyone-april-8-2026)**

The session covers data ingestion, embedding visualization, and model failure analysis. You will build workflows to identify dataset bias, find annotation errors, and select informative samples for training. Attendees leave with a framework for data centric AI for research and production pipelines, prioritizing data quality over pure model iteration.

What you'll learn

* **Structure unstructured data.** Map data and metadata into a queryable schema for images, videos, and point clouds.
* **Query datasets with the FiftyOne SDK**. Create complex views based on model predictions, labels, and custom tags. Use the FiftyOne to filter data based on logical conditions and confidence scores.
* **Visualize high dimensional embeddings**. Project features into lower dimensions to find clusters of similar samples. Identify data gaps and outliers using FiftyOne Brain.
* **Automate data curation.** Implement algorithmic measures to select diverse subsets for training. Reduce labeling costs by prioritizing high entropy samples.
* **Debug model performance.** Run evaluation routines to generate confusion matrices and precision recall curves. Visualize false positives and false negatives directly in the App to understand model failures.
* **Customize FiftyOne**. Build custom dashboards and interactive panels. Create specialized views for domain specific tasks.

**Prerequisites:**

* Working knowledge of Python and machine learning and/or computer vision fundamentals.
* All attendees will get access to the tutorials and code examples used in the workshop.
source: Meetup: Raleigh AI, ML & Computer Vision

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980073/1:b23ed0dc05ca3fd7fcacbd2b359ed2351dccabc3f8b0c37b9abd0b9655118a94/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage.

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country.

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare.

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

Join Black Voters Matter for our Warrant Clinic phonebank.

What is a warrant clinic? A warrant clinic is a ONE-DAY community-led grassroots program designed to help people eliminate their warrants due to misdemeanor offenses and traffic violations. It is an entry point to activism and civic engagement.

What will you do as a volunteer?: Help us reach out to residents and provide them with next steps for their warrant(s).

We provide training via Zoom. Which means, no matter what state you live in, you can volunteer!

How to prepare:
Fill out the volunteer agreement here - https://forms.office.com/r/TiaHyteJg9

3. Complete the training course and join us at the next phonebank.

title: Monthly Welcome, Community, and Project Meeting
description:
Code with the Carolinas - Triangle
Code with the Carolinas is a community of people working together as civic tech volunteers to improve wellbeing in North and South Carolina, and we invite you to join us for our biweekly online meetings!
Our meetings start with introductions, updates on old and new business, and status reports on current projects, emphasizing how you can get involved. All are welcome and no technical skills are needed.

**Typical Agenda**
Introductions
Organizational Updates
Project updates

* Active projects like Zoning Atlas
* Proposal development for new projects

Meetings are on the Jitsi platform and the link is provided before each meeting. If you will be joining on mobile, install the Jitsi app in advance [https://jitsi.org/downloads/](https://jitsi.org/downloads/) If you joined on mobile and your audio isn't working, it might be because you joined through a browser and not through the app.

Learn more at [[codewiththecarolinas.org](http://codewiththecarolinas.org/)]([http://codeforthecarolinas.org](http://codeforthecarolinas.org/)), [join our Slack](https://join.slack.com/t/codewiththecarolinas/shared_invite/zt-3i6ptx0pz-raH_7Zx4sX0wFUkYM9w3Zw), or reach out to [info@codewiththecarolinas.org](http://info@codewiththecarolinas.org/)

All activities of Code with the Carolinas, a project of [Social Good Fund](https://www.socialgoodfund.org/), follow our [Code of Conduct](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/0d32d5f905f8fd49d5e80395f8fd47f2b59ce3ef8b335f72/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTkx) and are subject to this [Volunteer Waiver of Liability](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/06dd62200e6b28426b137e0e6b2848fb901487014f72cd16/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTE2).

title: Aquatic WILD
description:
Aquatic Wild is a program offered through the NC Wildlife Resources Commission. The North Carolina Aquarium at Fort Fisher and NC Sea Grant are facilitating this workshop.

The Aquatic WILD workshop is for formal and non-formal educators interested in teaching people about aquatic wildlife and ecology. Aquatic WILD materials build upon developments in wildlife conservation needs as well as advances in instructional methodology in Pre-K through 12th grade education.

Participants in this 6-hour workshop receive the Aquatic WILD K-12 Curriculum & Activity Guide. The guide contains field investigations, In Step With STEM activity extensions, connections to wildlife careers, new activities on fish conservation and angling, and an increased focus on outdoor activities. Project Wild materials are provided free of charge by the NC Wildlife Resources Commission.

The workshop at the Carolina Beach State Park will have a marine focus. Participants are eligible for Criteria I credit of NC OEE Environmental Education Certification and school teachers receive CEU credit. Homework option available.

Location: Carolina Beach State Park

Cost: $25 per person

For additional workshop information, call 910-772-0546.

title: Social Media as Qualitative Data: A Design Framework
description:
Conversations that take place online – from learning management systems to Signal chats to Discord groups to Reddit support groups and Instagram influencing – can be valuable forms of evidence for qualitative researchers. In this workshop, participants will create their own research designs to investigate a phenomenon of interest, as evidenced through social media data, in a systematic and rigorous way. The Paulus and Wise research design decision framework will scaffold learners in how to identify an object of interest; articulate their philosophical and theoretical assumptions about how language functions online; create focused and relevant research questions; ensure methodological alignment across aspects of the study design; resolve ethical dilemmas around treating social media as data; extract and transform social media into a coherent dataset; select appropriate technologies for managing and analyzing data; perform a systematic narrative, discourse and/or thematic analysis; and establish the quality of the findings. Course material will be drawn from Looking for Learning, Insight and Transformation in Online Talk (Paulus & Wise, Routledge, 2019).

Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: Alternative Protein Symposium: Bridging Science and Society
description: Sustainable proteins are reshaping how we think about food production, nutrition and environmental stewardship. The Alternative Protein Symposium: Bridging Science and Society event will bring together students, researchers, industry leaders and policymakers to explore innovations in plant-based, fermentation-derived and cultivated protein systems.​ This two-day conference will focus on scientific advances and societal implications of transitioning to sustainable protein sources through keynote talks, panel discussions and student presentations. Please register.

Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. title: Right to Be's Bystander Intervention to Stop Online Harassment: How to Be an Ally When You Witness Abuse Online
description:
REGISTER HERE: https://righttobe.org/events/bystander-intervention-to-stop-online-harassment-how-to-be-an-ally-when-you-witness-abuse-online-7/

PEN America and Right to Be have partnered to provide training on how to intervene safely when you see a journalist attacked online. This training is also brought to you in partnership with the NYC Commission on Gender Equity, the Mayor’s Office of Equity and Racial Justice, and the NYC Commission on Gender Equity.

In this free, one-hour, interactive training, we’ll give you the tools you need to intervene safely and effectively in online abuse using Right To Be’s 5Ds of bystander intervention. First, we’ll talk through what online harassment is and how to identify abusive tactics. We’ll discuss the severe impact online abuse can have – on well being, livelihoods, and free expression – and how it’s drowning out voices that urgently need to be heard in public discourse. Then, we’ll share concrete strategies for how to distract, delegate, document, delay, or directly intervene in a way that is safe, effective, and centered on the needs of the person being targeted.
We’ll have time to practice so you can leave feeling more confident intervening the next time you see someone being abused online. We hope you’ll join us to learn how we can all band together to do your part to protect press freedom and defend the media from attacks on their independence, including online abuse and threats.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

description:

The Chatham County Historical Association needs your help in learning about the rich and colorful history of Siler City! They have facts and figures, but they need to hear your own memories of Siler to really understand one of Chatham County’s most important cities. Come share your memories, stories, photos, mementos, and other Siler City treasures. Light refreshments will be served. This event is free, and everyone is welcome! No registration is required.

This event takes place indoors. First Baptist Church in Siler City is ADA-accessible. No pets allowed. Please leave your furry friends at home. Free parking is available next to the church.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events. See more events hosted by The Chatham Historical Association.

title: April 9 - Workshop: Build a Visual Agent that can Navigate GUIs like Humans
description:
Raleigh AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision Meetup
This hands-on workshop provides a comprehensive introduction to building and evaluating visual agents for GUI automation using modern tools and techniques.

**Date, Time and Location**

April 9, 2026 at 9 AM Pacific
Online. **[Register for the Zoom](https://voxel51.com/events/visual-agents-what-it-takes-to-build-an-agent-that-can-navigate-guis-like-humans-april-9-2026)**

Visual agents that can understand and interact with graphical user interfaces represent a transformative frontier in AI automation. These systems combine computer vision, natural language understanding, and spatial reasoning to enable machines to navigate complex interfaces—from web applications to desktop software—just as humans do. However, building robust GUI agents requires careful attention to dataset curation, model evaluation, and iterative improvement workflows.

Participants will learn how to leverage [FiftyOne](https://github.com/voxel51/fiftyone), an open-source toolkit for dataset curation and computer vision workflows, to build production-ready GUI agent systems.

What You'll Learn:

* **Dataset Creation & Management:** How to structure, annotate, and load GUI interaction datasets using the COCO4GUI standardized format
* **Data Exploration & Analysis:** Using FiftyOne's interactive interface to visualize datasets, analyze action distributions, and understand annotation patterns
* **Multimodal Embeddings:** Computing embeddings for screenshots and UI element patches to enable similarity search and retrieval
* **Model Inference:** Running state-of-the-art models like Microsoft's GUI-Actor to predict interaction points from natural language instructions
* **Performance Evaluation:** Measuring model accuracy using standard metrics and normalized click distance to assess localization precision
* **Failure Analysis:** Investigating model failures through attention maps, error pattern analysis, and systematic debugging workflows
* **Data-Driven Improvement:** Tagging samples based on error types (attention misalignment vs. localization errors) to prioritize fine-tuning efforts
* **Synthetic Data Generation:** Using FiftyOne plugins to augment training data with synthetic task descriptions and variations

*About the Speaker*

[Harpreet Sahota](https://www.linkedin.com/in/harpreetsahota204/) is a hacker-in-residence and machine learning engineer with a passion for deep learning and generative AI. He’s got a deep interest in RAG, Agents, and Multimodal AI.
source: Meetup: Raleigh AI, ML & Computer Vision

title: Clear Health Communication: Strategies and Best Practices in Written and Verbal Communication
description:

This session will cover health literacy basics, as well as some strategies, resources, and best practices in using plain language to convey information by promoting and practicing clear written and verbal communication.

This workshop is offered by the UNC Health Sciences Library and led by Terri Ottosen.

title: Hybrid Lunchbox Talk: Plants in Pockets: A World Tour of Plants on Subtropical Coins
description:
With Austin Mast, Director, Robert K. Godfrey Herbarium and Institute for Digital Information & Sciences

Thursday, April 9, 2026

12:00 PM-1:00 PM ET

Hybrid - Join us in person or on Zoom!

Free; preregistration required. Register >

The subtropics harbor a diverse flora, from the forests of southeastern North America and southeast Asia to the scrubs of the world’s Mediterranean climates to the arid vegetation of most of the world’s deserts. This talk explores the plants of the subtropics and the people that appreciate them, ultimately addressing why collections of all types matter as we seek to understand our planet and deliver an increasingly more sustainable world to our kids and grandkids.

ABOUT THE SPEAKER

Austin Mast is a Professor in Florida State University’s Department of Biological Science where he serves as Director of both FSU’s Robert K. Godfrey Herbarium and FSU’s Institute for Digital Information & Scientific Communication. As a part of his leadership of iDigBio (NSF’s National Resource for Advancing Digitization of Biodiversity Collections), Austin thinks a lot about unlocking the expansive potential of collections like herbaria and gardens. As a founding board member for the Citizen Science Global Partnership, Austin thinks a lot about the opportunities offered to organizations like the UN Statistics Division and the International Union for the Conservation of Nature by the participatory sciences. Austin’s happy place is at the intersection of biodiversity, geography, history, economics, photography, conservation, and collections—a place that perhaps unexpectedly involves amassing a world coin collection numbering in the thousands.

title: Become a Lake James State Park Junior Ranger!
location: Lake James State Park- Paddy's Creek Visitor Center
description:
Calling all young explorers, nature lovers, and future park protectors! Join the Lake James State Park Rangers for an exciting Junior Ranger Program designed to help kids earn their official Junior Ranger Badge.

Kick things off by meeting our friendly Park Rangers, who will guide you through the Junior Ranger journey and share what it’s really like to work as a ranger- protecting wildlife, education, firefighting, keeping people safe, and helping visitors enjoy the park. Then the adventure is yours! Set out to explore Lake James State Park at your own pace while completing activities in the Junior Ranger Booklet along the way.

Once you’ve earned enough acorns (points), head back to the Visitor Center to take the Junior Ranger Oath and be officially sworn in as Lake James State Park’s newest Junior Ranger—badge included!

This program is fun, educational, and perfect for curious minds of all ages. Come learn, explore, and discover why Lake James State Park is such a special place!

title: TTRPG: Revolutionary Cumberland
description:
Travel back to 1770s–1780s Cumberland County, North Carolina, when tensions are rising and the American colonies are on the brink of revolution. In this immersive tabletop role-playing game, you’ll step into the shoes of local citizens navigating loyalty, rebellion and survival during a pivotal moment in history.

Explore real historical events, regional battles and political intrigue as you make choices that shape your story. Will you side with the Patriots, remain loyal to the Crown or try to survive somewhere in between?

No prior role-playing experience is necessary—just bring your imagination and your sense of adventure.

Registration is required by calling 910-483-7727, ext. 1365, or registering online.

description:
The Importance of Being Earnest, a Trivial Comedy for Serious People, is Oscar Wilde’s comedic play, which satirizes the conventions and manners of the late 19th century. Best friends Jack and Algernon each adopt fictional identities to escape social obligations. The plot unfolds with mistaken identities, romantic entanglements, clever wordplay and humorous social commentary.

The play's director is Danica Jenelle Jackson.

title: Birding with Wake Audubon
location: Williamson Farm and Nature Preserve, 4429 Old Country Mial Rd, Raleigh, 27610, United States
description:
Join TLC and Wake Audubon for this wonderful birding walk!
Our wonderful partners at Wake Audubon have joined TLC to lead a hike on the second Friday of each month at our Williamson Nature Preserve. Join this wonderful morning stroll and see what birds you can find. This hike will be just under 2 miles around the Two Pond Loop at the preserve. Please wear good walking shoes and layers, bring a water bottle, and binoculars if you have them. We suggest that you leave your furry friends at home for this hike as they may scare away wildlife viewing opportunities. This is a free event, but pre-registration is required.

If registration is full, please fill out the waitlist form here by April 8th at 10am.
Inclement Weather Policy: In the event of inclement weather, you will be contacted by email for a call on cancellation or postponement. At our Williamson and Brumley South Nature Preserves, due to the multi-use trail systems, we close the trails to the public if there has been rain to try and prevent erosion and detrimental impact. Please note that this event falls under this category and will be cancelled if the trails are closed. Please check your inboxes and spam folders prior to arriving!

title: Coffee Club Sponsored by Vtech Global Solutions
description: Thank you to Vtech Global Solutions for supporting this Coffee Club! Looking to expand your network and make valuable new connections? Join us for "Coffee Club" - a laid-back morning networking event. Chamber Coffee Club meets the 2nd Friday of every month from 8:00 - 9:00 am at The Loading Dock in Wake Forest. What to Expect: Complimentary coffee and light breakfast snacks Opportunity to network with professionals from diverse industries Casual, friendly atmosphere to make new contacts & swap business cards A fun raffle opportunity! This is the perfect chance to start your day meeting new people, sharing ideas, and growing your professional circle. Not a Wake Forest Area Chamber member? This event requires a personal invitation from a current member or staff. Reach out to us to get on the list!

title: Alternative Protein Symposium: Bridging Science and Society
description: Sustainable proteins are reshaping how we think about food production, nutrition and environmental stewardship. The Alternative Protein Symposium: Bridging Science and Society event will bring together students, researchers, industry leaders and policymakers to explore innovations in plant-based, fermentation-derived and cultivated protein systems.​ This two-day conference will focus on scientific advances and societal implications of transitioning to sustainable protein sources through keynote talks, panel discussions and student presentations. Please register.

title: Legends & Landmarks: Paintings and Drawings by James C. Horton
description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Horton, which includes over 40 original paintings and drawings that highlight historic scenes and places from around the region and country that help tell America's story. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. Art is available for purchase and an Artist's Reception will be held on February 27th (6-8pm). Visit bellamymansion.org/exhibits for more information or email info@bellamymansion.org if you have questions.

title: Prelude to Revolution: Halifax Resolves Days
description: Celebrate the 250th anniversary of the Halifax Resolves, the first official action by an entire colony recommending independence from Great Britain.  On Friday and Saturday, enjoy two days of living-history activities, historic weapons demonstrations, military drills, and interact with characters from Halifax’s past. Guest speakers will also give presentations on various topics. On Sunday, the living-history activities will continue.  In the afternoon, the NC Society Sons of the American Revolution will host their annual wreath-laying ceremony, and the Historical Halifax Restoration Association will host the annual Halifax Day ceremony, followed by a reception.  Free.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: NC State Accessibility Collaborative Meeting
description:
The NC State Accessibility Collaborative (NCStAC) is for faculty, staff and students interested in learning about, sharing and promoting accessibility across our university. NCStAC provides a venue for networking and community building, knowledge and information sharing, cross-departmental coordination and peer-to-peer support and collaboration in all areas related to accessibility.

View the agendas for our meetings and browse the NCStAC shared folder. A recording and slides will be added to both folders after each meeting. Join NCStAC  to be added to our Google group and receive the Zoom link for our meetings.

title: Modular Design and Automated Testing with R
description:
Research is relying on increasingly complex code. However, most academics have not received formal training in software development. This course will introduce participants to some of the best practices of professional software development. Participants will learn to structure their Python code into reusable functions and store those functions as separate scripts and/or packages. Participants will learn to write automated tests for their functions, to help confirm that results are as expected and to ensure that future changes to code do not cause unexpected changes to results.

We will also discuss how to create reproducible “environments” that record package versions in use to help avoid package version incompatibilities and ensure that results can be replicated even months or years after the original analysis.

Modular design is best paired with a version control system. We will discuss how modular design practices integrate with Git and Github, and how to set up “continuous integration” so that automated tests are run each time code is changed.

The course will primarily consist of a hands-on exercise to restructure a single long analysis file into functions and automated tests.

“Version Control with Git and Github” course is a prerequisite for this course.

Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: NC Public Librarian Certification Commission Meeting
location: North Carolina State Library of Accessible Books and Library Services, Room 308
description:
NC Public Librarian Certification Commission Meeting

The North Carolina Public Librarian Certification Commission was formed in 1936 to set minimum standards for public librarians to protect and maintain public library resources, to assure professional management and administration of library services, to meet the personnel requirements for State Aid administered by the State Library of NC, and to guarantee the best possible public library service to all North Carolinians. Commission meetings are held quarterly to review applications and discuss Certification policies and procedures.

For information about the NC Public Librarian Certification Commission, please visit https://slnc.info/PLCC.

title: UNC Wilderness Medicine M.A.R.C.H. Team 5k
description:
REGISTRATION  IS OPEN!

UNC 5K M.A.R.C.H. – Team Based MedWAR Style Race

Join the UNC Wilderness Medicine Program for the inaugural UNC 5K M.A.R.C.H. on April 11th, 2026! This MedWAR style adventure race places teams of four on a 5K course through the UNC Outdoor Education Center’s multi-use trails, where they will encounter five wilderness medicine scenarios based on the M.A.R.C.H. protocol. Scenarios emphasize teamwork, physical challenges, and applied medical skills.

This event is open exclusively to UNC affiliated participants (students, faculty, staff, etc.). Teams will be judged on scenario performance, teamwork, and style—speed alone won’t win the day.

Start/Finish & Parking Location:
UNC Disc Golf Course
910 Raleigh Road, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

Teams will begin via an 0800 rolling start, departing every few minutes. Registration requires a complete team of four, including your team name and all teammate names. We are planning for 20 total teams.

Friends and family are welcome at the start/finish area, where we will have booths and activities. Please bring your own food, water, and drinks. This event is rain or shine, though it may be cancelled for safety; refunds cannot be issued if cancellation occurs.

A waiver will be sent electronically before race day and must be signed by all team members prior to participation.

Questions? Contact Loboncaj@ad.unc.edu

Registration: https://apps2.research.unc.edu/events/index.cfm?event=events.go&key=CD43

Class size is limited to 20. Participants must be 16 or older.

title: MFA 2026 Thesis Exhibitions at Lump Gallery
description:
Department of Art and Art History

Lump Presents: UNC-Chapel Hill MFA Thesis Exhibitions

March 6th - March 29th:

INFRA ULTRA by CJ Murphy / Under the Thumb by Michelle Lisa Polissaint

Opening reception: March 6, 6-9 pm

April 3rd - April 26th:

Nepantla by Wendy Yamilett Moran / Growing Elsewhere: Ghorbatī (غربتی) by Rojano Mohammadzadeh / Blood, Sap, Dirt by Iris Moyan Wang

Opening reception: April 3, 6-9 pm

Lump Gallery is located at 505 S. Blount St., Raleigh, NC 27601

Opening Hours: Saturdays and Sundays, 1-5 pm, and by appointment.

Lump Gallery is pleased to present thesis exhibitions by UNC-Chapel Hill’s 2026 MFA graduating class.

At the conclusion of a rigorous, research-based two-year program, these artists consider the concept of place and their personal relationships to it. Each artist employs a range of media and responds to geographic, constructed, technological, and bodily autonomy. These relationships between place and personal narrative raise broader questions around positionality and society; communication and identity; and human impact. Revelation, purpose, and possibility guide each artist’s research-centered practices.

CJ Murphy creates works that render the intersection of technology, magic, and globalization material by reinvigorating found and discarded electrical waste. Michelle Lisa Polissaint draws on her experience in a strict religious community and deep research into the BITE method to discuss contemporary issues of indoctrination, high-control groups, and authoritarianism. Rojano Mohammadzadeh draws on Islamic prayer, talismanic imagery, text, and ritualized gestures to approach the immigrant experience through what she terms transplantation: survival through negotiating acceptance within a host body. Wendy Yamilett Morán’s practice explores the intersections of culture, realism, and spirituality through painting and ceramics, using their work to examine how identity, memory, and belonging are shaped within in-between spaces. Iris Moyan Wang is an interdisciplinary artist working primarily with ink painting and ceramics to create fluid and erotic hybridizations of bodies and botanicals that reimagine Chinese cultural heritage.

For press inquiries or other questions, please contact uncmfathesis@yahoo.com

### **-Masks are optional.**

title: Celebrating America's Playlist: The 250-Year Soundtrack of Freedom and Peace
description:
For 250 years, America has expressed its ideals of peace and freedom through music—and now, those songs are coming together in one powerful night. Celebrating America’s Playlist is a live concert that marks the nation’s Semiquincentennial with a soundtrack that spans from 1776 to today.

Set at the Fletcher Opera Theater in Raleigh, this event brings local choirs, soloists, and small groups to the stage—backed by a stellar live band and led by a visionary music director. Each performance is paired with short narration that connects the music to defining moments in U.S. history and to the enduring spirit of liberty that has shaped—and been shaped by—each generation.

Presented by Crabtree Rotary and your local Rotary clubs, this is more than a concert—it’s a celebration that lets the story of our nation sing through the voices of our community.

description: Come with us as we explore historic greenway trails in North Carolina by bicycle. This entry-level cycling adventure has no major hills and relatively low mileage over two days. Participants will need to know how to ride a bike.

title: Sensory Friendly Day
description:
Prior to opening to the general public, the museum will be open for a Sensory-Friendly event. Sensory Friendly Day begins at 9 a.m. You are welcome to stay as long as you wish, however the museum will open to the public at noon on Sensory Friendly Day.

Trained museum educators and volunteers will be stationed around the museum with specially designed interactive activities to create a meaningful experience at each exhibit. Sensory-friendly activities are available until 1pm.

Social Narratives and maps are available for download on our website prior to your visit. Visual supports, such as schedules and sensory-friendly maps will be available to help guide individuals through their preferred routes. Quiet/Calm down spaces will be available throughout the museum and highlighted on the provided venue map. Several sensory-friendly items will be available for sale in the Gift Station.

We hope to provide a fun and engaging experience for individuals of all ages, filled with opportunities to learn, interact, and enjoy the many exhibits that we have to offer.

title: Bike Maintenance Class and Group Ride
description:
Every second Sunday, get your biking fix with the Greenway Gear Collective's Bike Maintenance Class and Group Ride. Their 10 a.m. monthly basic maintenance class is a great way to learn the essentials of bike care and tune-ups. Each class is shaped by the needs of those who show up with no two being the same.

Then at noon, you can join a themed group ride starting and ending at The Bike Library container on the shores of Lake Raleigh. Bring your own bike or rent one of theirs. Feel free to show up for the class, the group ride or both.

While both of these events are drop-in accessible and encouraged, signing up in advance for the class or pre-filling out the waiver for the ride is always greatly appreciated.

title: Prelude to Revolution: Halifax Resolves Days
description: Celebrate the 250th anniversary of the Halifax Resolves, the first official action by an entire colony recommending independence from Great Britain.  On Friday and Saturday, enjoy two days of living-history activities, historic weapons demonstrations, military drills, and interact with characters from Halifax’s past. Guest speakers will also give presentations on various topics. On Sunday, the living-history activities will continue.  In the afternoon, the NC Society Sons of the American Revolution will host their annual wreath-laying ceremony, and the Historical Halifax Restoration Association will host the annual Halifax Day ceremony, followed by a reception.  Free.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Annual Sims Lecture: Native Swagger: Turning Heads with Native Plants
description:
Join us for our Annual Evelyn McNeill Sims Native Plant Lecture: Native Swagger: Turning Heads with Native Plants with Andrea DeLong-Amaya, Horticulture Educator at the Lady Bird Johnson Wildflower Center!

Sunday, April 12, 2026 5:30 PM ET

Hybrid - Join us in person or on Zoom!

Free; preregistration required. Register >

Native plants are resilient ecological powerhouses and anchor us to our botanical heritage. But too often, they’re dismissed as messy or weedy. It’s time to change the narrative! In this talk, we’ll explore how to use native plants to create gardens that are true people-pleasers. From wild to formal designs, lawns to containers, sun to shade, wildlife gardens to water gardens, even edible gardens — you’ll see how natives provide habitat while also serving as the basis for stunning landscapes. Whether you’re already a native-plant champion or just native-curious, you’ll come away with ideas to elevate your garden and give native plants the starring role they deserve.

ABOUT THE SPEAKER

For over three decades, Andrea DeLong-Amaya has been living her life’s mission to honor the natural world through presenting and teaching classes about the horticulture, ecology, and importance of native plants in planned landscapes.

Over the prior 21 years, she served as the Center’s Director of Horticulture, overseeing the nine acres of Texas native plant gardens, and nursery programs. She’s been a staff member since 1998 and has over 30 years of experience with Texas native plants in horticulture, ecology and garden design. Recently, she has taken on a new role at the Wildflower Center focusing more on teaching and advancing interpretation in the gardens.

She has contributed numerous gardening articles to publications such as Taunton’s Fine Gardening, Rodale’s Organic Gardening, American Public Gardens Association’s Public Garden, Neil Sperry’s Gardens and e-Gardens, Texas Gardener and Wildflower (the Center’s member magazine). Her regionally best-selling book, the Texas Native Plant Primer (Timber Press), was released in March of 2025.

DeLong-Amaya has appeared numerous times on Central Texas Gardener, PBS’s long-running television program, and was a two-time guest on WNYC’s Science Friday. Jennifer Jewell featured Andrea’s work in her 2020 book, “The Earth in Her Hands: 75 Extraordinary Women Working in the World of Plants,” and interviewed DeLong-Amaya on the podcast Cultivating Place. The Scott Arboretum of Swarthmore College honored DeLong-Amaya with the prestigious 2025 Scott Garden & Horticulture Award in March 2025.

ABOUT THE SIMS LECTURE

Every spring, the Garden offers a lecture focused on native plants and their conservation and ecology. The lecture series was initiated in 2000 with a gift from then Botanical Garden Foundation Board member Nancy Preston. Mrs. Preston wanted to honor her mother, Evelyn McNeill Sims, on the occasion of her 90th birthday. Born in Lumberton, North Carolina, Mrs. Sims was educated at UNC-Greensboro and later moved to Kingsport, Tennessee, with her husband and daughter. Wildflower excursions in the mountains surrounding Kingsport were a favorite activity for Mrs. Sims, who eventually became a volunteer guide at Bays Mountain Nature Park.

title: MFA 2026 Thesis Exhibitions at Lump Gallery
description:
Department of Art and Art History

Lump Presents: UNC-Chapel Hill MFA Thesis Exhibitions

March 6th - March 29th:

INFRA ULTRA by CJ Murphy / Under the Thumb by Michelle Lisa Polissaint

Opening reception: March 6, 6-9 pm

April 3rd - April 26th:

Nepantla by Wendy Yamilett Moran / Growing Elsewhere: Ghorbatī (غربتی) by Rojano Mohammadzadeh / Blood, Sap, Dirt by Iris Moyan Wang

Opening reception: April 3, 6-9 pm

Lump Gallery is located at 505 S. Blount St., Raleigh, NC 27601

Opening Hours: Saturdays and Sundays, 1-5 pm, and by appointment.

Lump Gallery is pleased to present thesis exhibitions by UNC-Chapel Hill’s 2026 MFA graduating class.

At the conclusion of a rigorous, research-based two-year program, these artists consider the concept of place and their personal relationships to it. Each artist employs a range of media and responds to geographic, constructed, technological, and bodily autonomy. These relationships between place and personal narrative raise broader questions around positionality and society; communication and identity; and human impact. Revelation, purpose, and possibility guide each artist’s research-centered practices.

CJ Murphy creates works that render the intersection of technology, magic, and globalization material by reinvigorating found and discarded electrical waste. Michelle Lisa Polissaint draws on her experience in a strict religious community and deep research into the BITE method to discuss contemporary issues of indoctrination, high-control groups, and authoritarianism. Rojano Mohammadzadeh draws on Islamic prayer, talismanic imagery, text, and ritualized gestures to approach the immigrant experience through what she terms transplantation: survival through negotiating acceptance within a host body. Wendy Yamilett Morán’s practice explores the intersections of culture, realism, and spirituality through painting and ceramics, using their work to examine how identity, memory, and belonging are shaped within in-between spaces. Iris Moyan Wang is an interdisciplinary artist working primarily with ink painting and ceramics to create fluid and erotic hybridizations of bodies and botanicals that reimagine Chinese cultural heritage.

title: Closed Loop Cycle Tour
description: Join NC State Dining for an immersive Earth Month experience as we explore the journey of food from farm to table. We'll meet outside of Fountain Dining Hall at 9:30 a.m. From there, we'll take a bus to the NC State Compost Facility and Research Cooperative to learn how food waste from the dining halls is turned into a nutrient-rich soil amendment. Our second stop will be at the Agroecology Education Farm to see how that compost is utilized and how food is grown on campus. Lastly, we'll close the loop and enjoy lunch at Fountain Dining Hall, featuring some of the produce from the Agroecology Education Farm.

title: The Art and Science of Plant Care
description:
Caring for plants is both an art and a science — part intuition, part know-how. Every gardener, from beginner to expert, faces the same essential questions: When should I prune? How much should I water? What’s the best way to plant, fertilize or repot? In this class, Bryce Lane will bring decades of horticultural expertise to demystify these everyday tasks, showing you not just what to do but why it works.

Over eight weeks, you’ll explore the fundamental practices that keep gardens thriving, learning how to perform each one with confidence and purpose. You’ll uncover the science behind good technique — understanding how soil, water, nutrients and plant biology interact — while also developing the practical judgment that comes only with experience.

Each session will focus on one key aspect of plant care, building a complete toolkit for growing healthier, more resilient plants in any garden setting.

description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Horton, which includes over 40 original paintings and drawings that highlight historic scenes and places from around the region and country that help tell America's story. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Maximizing Your Research Impact
description:
During this workshop, participants will be provided useful tips and tools for optimizing the reach of their work. Attendees will gain a series of strategic skills to help ensure their published research reaches a wide audience and its research impact can be tracked.

Topics covered will include:

Research impact measurement optionsOptimizing abstract writing for database discoverabilityWriting a plain language summary of a studyBenefits of ORCID and how to set up an ORCIDUnderstanding open access publishing and author rightsThis workshop is offered by the UNC Health Sciences Library and led by Emma Barrett-Catton

title: Durham Social Hack Night
description:
Triangle Developers
Hey tech folks, come out and hang at Hi-Wire starting at 6pm! Hack Night is meant to be a social evening for folks in tech. It's less about networking and more about community.

**Look for the Hack Night tabletop sign** and nerds wearing name tags to find us. If the weather is nice, we'll probably be outside.

Help folks feel welcome by using the **"Pac Man Rule"** \- when standing with a group of people \(usually in a circle\)\, leave some space open so that another person can easily join\.

See our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/TriangleDevsSlack/policies/blob/master/code-of-conduct.md). To summarize: be nice or leave. No tolerance for harassment or oppression. In addition, since alcohol is present at these events, please be mindful of your consumption and respect other people's choices on whether or not to consume.

**FAQs**
*Do folks hack at Hack Night? Should I bring my computer?*
This is more of a night for hackers than an actual hack night. You're welcome to bring a laptop, but most people will only be socializing.

*What are the beverage options? Are there any non-alcoholic drinks?*
Hi-Wire Brewing offers many types of beers including a few non-alcoholic options plus wine and some liquor. They usually have a happy hour special on the first few beers on the menu. In the same complex are Cugino Forno which has additional drink options, and Two Roosters which has excellent ice cream.

*Are there any drink specials?*
If Hack Night is on a Tuesday, Hi-Wire usually has $5 pints for their flagship beers. Look for the list at the counter.

*Do people eat snacks/dinner during the meetup?*
Sometimes folks will order a pizza or salad from Cugino Forno. I sometimes grab an ice cream from Two Roosters across the way. Most people eat before or after but it's no big deal - whatever you prefer and are most comfortable with.

*How can I connect with the developer community?*
Go to [triangledevelopers.com](https://triangledevelopers.com/) to find the Discord and Slack info.

title: *VIRTUAL* SF/F Group
description:
Durham Writers Group
**Zoom Info Below Description**

This sub-group focuses on Science Fiction and Fantasy, so if you have anything that falls under that category — be it short story, poem, novel-in-progress or screenplay — then come! We'd love to hear from you.

Our format allows each reader 15 minutes to present their work... followed by approximately 20 minutes of feedback from the other attendees. To ensure a fair opportunity to respond, each attendee is limited to 1 minute of feedback time. (We usually get through three readers.)

If a reader elects to post their work in advance (NOT a requirement!), members will be able to access that work at our Google Group:
https://groups.google.com/d/forum/durham-sff-writers-group

We ask all members to provide a warning, both on the Google Group and during the meeting, if their story features content that some readers might find upsetting. This includes descriptions of sexual violence.

Readers are:
•
•
•

Backup Readers are:
•
•
•

Deep Backup:

***

***We meet every Tuesday at the same time, but alternate between meeting In-Person and on Zoom. Members may attend both formats, if desired, but readers will be listed separately under the In-Person or Zoom weeks.***

title: Global Film Series: On Becoming a Guinea Fowl
location: James B. Hunt Jr. Library, Auditorium
description:
Join the Global Film Series, a collaboration between the NC State University Libraries and NC State’s Office of Global Engagement, for a screening of On Becoming a Guinea Fowl.

On an empty road in the middle of the night, Shula stumbles across the body of her uncle. As funeral proceedings begin, Shula and her cousins bring to light the buried secrets of their middle-class Zambian family.

The movie runtime is 1 hour, 39 minutes. The film is in English and Bemba with English subtitles.

Dr. Haddy Njie, associate teaching professor in International Studies at NC State, will introduce the film.

The Global Film Series is a collaboration between the NC State University Libraries and NC State’s Office of Global Engagement. This screening is in partnership with the NC State Department of International Studies and Africana Studies.

title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979985/1:df6d648ae6178962cd9fa118925e7246da03f5a1cff98b9de823dc83faeafbe4/

How to join:
Hi there,

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.**

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools.

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too):

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son.

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team:

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...

https://www.mobilize.us/raceforward/event/596321/
