title: Taller Mensual de MamásConPoder
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367860/1:f8618d5aa26bbf8fd6bfe93a7b40aefa4cc9ec3f4582ad5f9c00dd016abd23c6/

How to join:
Únete a la Reunión en este link: https://momsrising.zoom.us/j/84073901424

Te invitamos a participar en nuestro Taller de Salud Mental este miercoles 29 de Octubre a las 5:30 Hora del Pacifico, impartido por la **Dra. Sandra Martínez**, psicóloga y psicoterapeuta con más de 20 años de experiencia brindando apoyo emocional con un enfoque empático, profesional y humano. Taller de Salud Mental,

**Este evento es gratuito y lo único que se necesita para entrar es tener una cuenta de Zoom. **

Este espacio está diseñado especialmente para la comunidad latina en EE. UU., con el propósito de ofrecer recursos prácticos y estrategias útiles para mejorar nuestra calidad de vida y afrontar con fortaleza estos tiempos de cambio e incertidumbre.

Durante el taller, hablaremos sobre temas esenciales como:

- Bienestar emocional: la importancia de cuidar nuestra salud mental.

- Manejo del estrés, miedo y ansiedad: herramientas y técnicas para recuperar el equilibrio.

- Creación de espacios seguros: cómo cultivar la salud desde nosotros mismos.

- Acompañamiento a nuestros hijos y comunidad: cómo, como madres y líderes, podemos apoyar, resistir y manejar el miedo.

- Educación y apoyo en crisis: cómo acompañar de manera efectiva a quienes están pasando por momentos difíciles.

Además, contaremos con una sesión de preguntas y respuestas con la Dra. Sandra Martínez, donde podrás resolver tus dudas y compartir tus experiencias.

**Regístrate ahora para recibir notificaciones y recordatorios sobre el próximo encuentro.**

¡Tu bienestar emocional también importa!

**NOTA IMPORTANTE: Tomamos muy en serio tu privacidad y toda tu información compartida por aquí o por cualquier otro medio con MamásConPoder está protegida y no se comparte con nadie más.**

description:

The group meets over three sessions, twice each semester. Lunch will be provided. The sessions are below.

title: Centennial SoundBites: Live Music and Lunch
description:
Take a lunch break and soak in the sounds of local artists at Centennial SoundBites, a relaxing lunchtime music series featuring 321 Coffee, local food trucks and some incredible local artists. Whether you come for the tunes, the food, the coffee or just to take a moment and enjoy yourself, we've got you covered.

You'll discover a new performer every Wednesday afternoon with artist coordination by Oak City Music Collective. We'll feature a range of fresh sounds from local solo and duet projects from the Triangle and beyond.

Free parking is available at the Oval West Parking Deck. To park there for free, validate your license plate using our QR code upon arrival.

title: Languishing, Flourishing, and Your Mental Health
description:
Over the past few years, employees have been asked to do more, do their work differently and do better in new and challenging ways, all while managing family life and personal responsibilities that seem never-ending. The feeling of languishing or “will this ever end?” abounds throughout the workforce and can have a huge impact on your mental health. Working on our mental health need not be reserved just for those who are in crisis or who are faced with an official diagnosis: Mental health matters for everyone. This interactive session provides more details and helpful ideas on how to move from languishing through work and life to flourishing.

Workshop Objectives
This training will help you:
• Understand the concepts of languishing and flourishing
• Reflect on your current mental wellness status
• Describe three concrete approaches to move from languishing to flourishing
• Identify and action plan to implement within the next 30 days to move toward flourishing

Click on Carolina Talent registration link and select the “Request” button to automatically enroll.

title: From AI to ROI: Turning Pilots Into Scalable Impact
description:
Data Science Raleigh
Every enterprise has its share of AI proof-of-concepts—many promising, few enduring. This webinar goes beyond the hype to explore what it actually takes to move from experimental AI pilots to sustained, enterprise-wide value. We’ll dig into the operational, organizational, and architectural levers that help AI investments move out of the sandbox and into the business mainstream.

Through expert discussion and real-world examples, we’ll explore the friction points that stall scaling efforts—and what forward-thinking leaders are doing to overcome them. If you're leading or influencing AI adoption in your organization, this session is about turning progress into performance.

**Key Takeaways:**

1️⃣ **Real Product Experience:** See full workflows in motion—not just the highlights.

2️⃣ **Operational Readiness:** What’s required in terms of data infrastructure, governance, and cross-functional collaboration to support scaled AI.

3️⃣ **Organizational Design:** How roles, incentives, and workflows evolve when AI becomes a core operating capability.

4️⃣ **Sustaining Value:** Strategies for continuous iteration, observability, and measuring long-term ROI from AI investments.

**[Register here](https://hubs.ly/Q041vh4c0)**

title: Ranger Workshop - Pollinator Power
description: From butterflies to birds to bees, learn about different pollinator species of the High Country and how each one plays a part in our environment! Get some tips and tricks on how to welcome some of these species to your yard and make your yard native pollinator friendly! This program will be held at the Seven Devils Community Center as part of their Ranger Workshop Series.

title: Game for Growth
description: Come take a break from studying. The Counseling Center's Game for Growth allows students to engage in various activities, such as board games, role-playing games and virtual reality to promote emotional wellness, social skills and personal growth. The activities occur in a supportive environment that encourages self-expression through play, helping students build confidence and develop healthy relationships with peers. All students are welcome to participate as often as they desire.

title: ERA - Wildflower Wednesday - Guided Hike - Confluence Natural Area
location: 4214 Highland Farm Rd, Hillsborough, NC 27278, USA
description:
Registration is required - Tickets drop on March 1st at 8:00am!
Price: $4
*Ticket prices directly support the work of the Eno River Association. Thank you for your support!

Celebrate Earth Month with us for this special Wildflower Wednesday hike series at the Confluence! Join three guided hikes every other Wednesday in April to explore various trails at the Confluence, based on where the best spring blooms are at the time. The Confluence is a magical place for spring wildflowers!

Meet at the pavilion beside the parking lot. This hike will take approximately two hours (sometimes less, sometimes more). Please feel free to bring water and snacks. Wear comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing and shoes. Well-behaved dogs are welcome as long as they are under your control and on a 6-foot leash. Restrooms are available on-site.

The address is 4214 Highland Farm Rd, Hillsborough, NC 27278.
From Hillsborough take Hwy 86 North away from town 1.6 miles from US70. Turn left on Coleman Loop 1.1 miles. Turn left on Highland Farm Rd 2.4 miles. The Confluence is on the left. There is a large sign and a gravel driveway. From northern Orange County take Hwy 86 south after passing Walnut Grove Church Rd take the first right on Kenion Rd 1.7 miles. Turn right on Highland Farm Rd 0.6 miles. The Confluence is on the left. There is a large sign and a gravel driveway.

title: Lean Library Workspace (Sciwheel): Create Citations and Bibliographies
description:
This workshop covers the basic features of Lean Library Workspace (fka: Sciwheel), a tool available for free through UNC, that can be used to organize and annotate references, and create citations and bibliographies. It will focus on setting up your Lean Library Workspace account, using Lean Library Workspace to organize and annotate references, and generating citations and bibliographies in Word. It is structured for new to beginning users.

This workshop is offered by the UNC Health Sciences Library and led by Sydney Larson.

title: 3rd Annual Legal History Lecture
description:
Please join the Department of History at the third annual Legal History Lecture. This public event is part of the department’s legal history programming and honors the memory of Jonathan Ocko, who served as head of the history department and was a department faculty member. Ocko was also a Chinese legal historian.

This event will feature Marcus Gadson, a UNC-Chapel Hill School of Law associate professor and a Harvard Law graduate who is a leading authority on state constitutions. He has published work in top-tier journals, including the New York University, Michigan and Georgetown law reviews. Gadson is the author of Sedition: How America’s Constitutional Order Emerged From Violent Crisis. Previously, he practiced at Steptoe & Johnson and clerked for Sixth Circuit U.S. Court of Appeals Judge Bernice Donald.

description:

### Join Our Greater Triangle Regional Coalition Meeting

- 📅 **When**: 3rd Wednesday of every month, 6:00 – 7:00 PM
- 📍 **Where**: Virtual (Online)

Democracy NC’s Greater Triangle region covers **Wake, Harnett, and Johnston counties.**

**We come together to:**

- Build strong grassroots leadership
- Equip people to take action
- Serve “the good of the whole”

Whether you’re brand new or already engaged, this space is for learning, sharing, and organizing for **a movement built of, by, and for the people**, grounded in equity, solidarity, and justice.

**A note from your Greater Triangle Regional Managing Organizer:**
I’m passionate about building community, amplifying voices, and creating spaces where people can be heard and find their role in supporting civic engagement and protecting our democracy. This work keeps me grounded, and I’m excited to invite you to be part of it.

**Join us, share your voice, and help shape the work ahead!**

How to prepare:
Thank you for signing up to attend our monthly coalition meeting!

Each month, you’ll get a reminder with details on what we’ll be discussing and the actions we can take together. We’re excited to stay connected and work alongside you to make an impact.

Hope to see you online!

— Sugelema Lynch

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980074/1:b041eb34d713fa0181836c2a4400c7d26526c8244d26b4e768fd83cf0f55c267/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage.

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country.

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare.

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Architecture Lecture: Andrew E. Thompson, Preservation NJ
location: AIA NC Center for Architecture and Design
description:
Please join the College of Design for the spring 2026 architecture lecture series. This is the AIA Triangle Design Award Jury Chair Lecture.

Andrew E. Thompson, Preservation NJ

Serving in his first term on the Preservation New Jersey Board is Andrew E. Thompson, who has been in the field of architecture for 30 years. His education includes City University of New York, the Pratt Institute, and Columbia University.

Andrew’s extensive career includes designing structural components for the Arleigh Burke Class Navy Destroyer vessel; the Port Authority – working on projects in Tower One of the World Trade Center, Newark Airport, and the Air Train for JFK airport; Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center where he served as the Chief Architect; and the City University of New York, where he was responsible for building their first new community college in 40 years. In 2013, Andrew came to New Jersey taking the position of County Architect for Passaic County.

title: Read Smart Author Event: The Legend of Wyatt Outlaw
description: Join Belle Boggs, professor in the Department of English at NC State, and Sylvester Allen Jr. as they discuss their new book, The Legend of Wyatt Outlaw: From Reconstruction Through Black Lives Matter. Outlaw’s story was one of Black success — he was a Union League leader, business owner and the first Black town constable and commissioner in Graham, a small town in North Carolina’s Alamance County. But in 1870, the Ku Klux Klan murdered Outlaw, setting off a dramatic series of events. Allen and Boggs both sought to discover why Outlaw had been erased from mainstream history books. In The Legend of Wyatt Outlaw, they share what they found in artful detail and connect Outlaw’s story to the violence against Black people in Alamance and throughout the United States, from Reconstruction through Jim Crow, the civil rights era and Black Lives Matter.

title: OpenCourts Project - Biweekly Sync
description:
Code with the Carolinas - Triangle
Join volunteers at Code with the Carolinas for the biweekly meeting about OpenCourts, a project to build an open data portal for North and South Carolina legal data.. Starting with aggregate datasets from South Carolina courts, volunteers will be working to adopt this data (and our site being constructed to host it) to standards and measures more usable for accountability and analysis. These principles of analysis are drawn from guidance provided by the legal accountability organization [Measures for Justice](https://measuresforjustice.org/), whose limited work in the Carolinas we are trying to renew and expand.

This effort is building toward multi-state data portal that catalogues and publishes trusted, permissively-licensed court records to ensure open legal data remains a **public good** accessible to everyone. ️

Anyone welcome whether an already identified volunteer on the project or just curious. We do encourage new participants to fill out this [interest form](https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=DQSIkWdsW0yxEjajBLZtrQAAAAAAAAAAAAO__cyETRVUOTZDSDlCQ0cxRThFMEVUUFNRWkZES08zSy4u&route=shorturl).

location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Hybrid Lunchbox Talk: If Not Us, Who? Rebuilding Botanical Capacity for the Southeast
description:
With Joey Shaw, Distinguished Service and UC Foundation Professor, University of Tennessee at Chattanooga

Thursday, April 16, 2026

12:00 PM-1:00 PM ET

Hybrid - Join us in person or on Zoom!

Free; preregistration required: https://reg.learningstream.com/reg/event_page.aspx...

The accelerating loss of biodiversity, coupled with a decline in botany programs, organismal biology and plant identification skills, has created an urgent need to rebuild botanical capacity. Botanical work depends on both traditional skills, like field observation and specimen identification, but we also need contemporary tools that improve worker efficiency, accessibility, and scale. The development of digital infrastructures such as digitized herbarium specimen portals, online plant atlases, keys (books and digital), and data-driven conservation metrics allows a small number of trained botanists to have outsized impact, supporting research, conservation, land management, and education.

This presentation explores how the deliberate integration of tool development, student training, and public outreach can strengthen the next generation of human infrastructure needed to document, understand, advocate for, and protect plant diversity.

ABOUT THE SPEAKER

Joey Shaw is a Distinguished Service and UC Foundation Professor at the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga, where he has been on the faculty since 2005. He earned his M.S. and Ph.D. in Botany from the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Dr. Shaw’s career integrates herbarium science, floristics, plant conservation, molecular systematics, teaching, and public engagement, with a focus on building tools and communities of people that advance botanical research and conservation.

title: Geospatial Forum: Global Data for Local Action
description:
The Geospatial Forum will host a lecture by Ted Wong, a data scientist and research associate on the Urban Analytics team at the World Resources Institute's Ross Center for Sustainable Cities.

The World Resources Institute (WRI) is a large environmental nongovernmental organization that provides geospatial data and analyses to local governments to support sustainable urban planning. This talk will examine how WRI builds and deploys data products supporting city-level planning and decision-making for extreme heat, urban biodiversity and decarbonization. We will discuss theories of change, user research, project-level business models, strategies for mainstreaming, representation of uncertainty and all the technical considerations behind our data products.

title: Celebrating Our Colonial Roots - Online Plant Sale
location: Pick up 5/1/26 at Greenhouses behind Building N
description: The Brunswick County Master Gardeners Volunteer Association will hold an online plant sale starting on April 17, 2026 and running until April 24, 2026 with pick up day on May 1, 2026. This event celebrates the important colonial history of Brunswick County  through education on herbs, flowers, vines, shrubs and small trees which thrive here. Plants featured are those which our Colonial ancestors would have encountered along our coast and the Cape Fear River which are native to our region and support pollinators. Also featured will be plants from seeds which colonists brought with them from Europe and plants which recognize the contributions and life celebrations of the Gullah Geechee people who were enslaved in Africa to work the rice plantations along the Cape Fear River. This online plant sale celebrates the cultural traditions  found in gardening in our coastal region of the State.

title: The Psychology Behind Saving Money and Other Good Financial Habits
description:
Most people can honestly say they believe it’s important to spend within their means and to save money for the future. Unfortunately, good intentions are often at odds with how our mind works. For example, many people want to save for the future but have no motivation to because they have not imagined that future. This course addresses the psychology behind saving money and adopting good financial habits. It will examine recent research related to how and why people save vs. spend and identify strategies for participants to change their financial mindset and develop new habits.

Workshop Objectives
At the end of this workshop, you will be able to:
• Understand why some people save for the future and other spend for the moment
• Identify non-productive financial patterns and habits
• Recognize the mental and behavioral habits of people who save
• Create a compelling financial future to motivate behavior change
• Understand how to improve the capacity to delay gratification while increasing enjoyment
• Identify seven simple tools to help change spending habits

title: Terrain Dance Project Concert: Themes of Gathering
description:
NC State’s Terrain Dance Project will present Themes of Gathering, a collection of new site-specific choreographic works at The Corner on Centennial Campus at 2 p.m. April 17 and 7 p.m. April 18. The Terrain Dance Project is a site-specific dance company dedicated to the creation and performance of choreographed and improvised dance works in nontraditional spaces, including dance film projects.

This interdisciplinary performance investigates The Corner as a site of gathering and identity, featuring live musicians and choreography by co-director Amy Love Beasley, guest artists Jill Guyton Nee and Anabelle Scarborough and NC State senior Annabelle Sharp. Select works explore the intersection of dance and science, inspired by work by NC State's Gage Lab and the North Carolina Sweetpotato Commission. This project is supported by an arts and science grant from the Office of Interdisciplinary Programs and Arts NC State.

title: Let's Go Fishing
location: Lake James State Park-Catawba River Access
description:
Join us for a relaxing morning fishing program at Lake James State Park – Catawba River Access, perfect for families and first-time anglers alike! Bring your kids, your favorite fishing rods, and any tackle you have—we’ll also have a limited number of rods and tackle available to loan.

We’ll meet at the picnic shelter, then head together to the jetty, where there’s plenty of space to spread out and fish. Spend the morning enjoying the calm water, learning a few fishing tips, and sharing quality time with loved ones while making lasting memories—plus, you just might reel in a fish or two!

Whether you’re teaching your child to fish for the first time or simply enjoying a peaceful morning outdoors, this program is a great way to connect with nature and each other.

All ages welcome. No experience necessary! All NCWRC Laws must be followed such as having a valid NC fishing license.🎣

title: Celebrating Our Colonial Roots - Online Plant Sale
location: Pick up 5/1/26 at Greenhouses behind Building N
description: The Brunswick County Master Gardeners Volunteer Association will hold an online plant sale starting on April 17, 2026 and running until April 24, 2026 with pick up day on May 1, 2026. This event celebrates the important colonial history of Brunswick County  through education on herbs, flowers, vines, shrubs and small trees which thrive here. Plants featured are those which our Colonial ancestors would have encountered along our coast and the Cape Fear River which are native to our region and support pollinators. Also featured will be plants from seeds which colonists brought with them from Europe and plants which recognize the contributions and life celebrations of the Gullah Geechee people who were enslaved in Africa to work the rice plantations along the Cape Fear River. This online plant sale celebrates the cultural traditions  found in gardening in our coastal region of the State.

description:

It will include hands-on STEM activities, STEM educational and career pathways, STEM content, skills and research, STEM professionals with disabilities and community resources.

title: Yoga In Nature™ ALL LEVELS YOGA at For Garden's Sake
description:
Yoga In Nature™, NC
**So excited to be back and offer another Yoga In Nature™ event at For Garden's Sake!!!** ***\*$25/person paid at signup in advance to secure your spot***\*.\* \*\*\*Pay by Venmo @Darlene-Genander (0862 if asked for 4 digits) or by PayPal dgenander@yogainnature.com Message me for Zelle or Cash options.

Come join us in the open field alongside the pond for a 60 minute vinyasa flow to yin practice for **ALL LEVELS and first timers.** We will gather as a community in a yoga practice that will focus on flowing with our breath as we move our bodies and connect with nature, being guided with all o four senses. No experience necessary, just an open mind:) We will plant seeds for our growth on and off our mats. You will also receive a small gift at this event!

Bring a friend! Gift someone special who loves yoga and/or plants. Stay for hot tea following the practice. Wander the grounds of the garden center's indoor and outdoor plants, shrubs and trees for your own home or inspiration. Also, take a look at their potting shed and visit with the goats and ducks on the property.

Bring your own yoga mat and/or towel, water bottle, and whatever you may need for your practice. Sunscreen, if needed, as we will be in open field under the sun.

Tentative rain date will be April 19, if needed. If there is a rain date, and you are unable to make it, you will be reimbursed.

title: Puppy Savvy Hike
location: Brumley North Nature Preserve, 3620 Old State Highway 10, Chapel Hill, NC, 27514, United States
description:
Bring your furry friend on an exploration of Brumley Nature Preserve!
Come hike with other dog lovers, practice training on the trail, and learn how to safeguard the nature conservation land we hold dear. The hike is led by Barbara Shumannfang, who is a TLC trail guide and dog trainer at Puppy Savvy, LLC.

There is a maximum of one dog per handler. This hike is for adults only.  Dogs must be non-reactive and on a 6ft fixed-length (not retractable) leash at all times. This is a free event, but preregistration is required.

If registration is full, please fill out the waitlist form here by April 16th at noon.

title: ERA - Guided Forest Therapy Hike - Confluence Natural Area
location: 4214 Highland Farm Rd, Hillsborough, NC 27278, USA
description:
Registration is required - Tickets drop on March 1st at 8:00am!
Price: $4
*Ticket prices directly support the work of the Eno River Association. Thank you for your support!

Forest Therapy (sometimes known as Forest Bathing or Shinrin-Yoku) broadly means taking in the forest atmosphere with all of one's senses. Not simply a walk in the woods; it is the conscious and contemplative practice of being immersed in the sights, sounds, and smells of the forest. During your walk, Aimee Vandemark will guide you through invitations to awaken your senses, slow down, be present, and reconnect with the nature and rhythm of your surroundings.

Meet at the pavilion beside the parking lot. This hike will take approximately two hours (sometimes less, sometimes more). Please feel free to bring water and snacks. Wear comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing and shoes. Well-behaved dogs are welcome as long as they are under your control and on a 6-foot leash. Restrooms are available on-site.

The address is 4214 Highland Farm Rd, Hillsborough, NC 27278.
From Hillsborough take Hwy 86 North away from town 1.6 miles from US70. Turn left on Coleman Loop 1.1 miles. Turn left on Highland Farm Rd 2.4 miles. The Confluence is on the left. There is a large sign and a gravel driveway. From northern Orange County take Hwy 86 south after passing Walnut Grove Church Rd take the first right on Kenion Rd 1.7 miles. Turn right on Highland Farm Rd 0.6 miles. The Confluence is on the left. There is a large sign and a gravel driveway.

title: Out of the Darkness Campus Walk at UNC Chapel Hill
description:
The UNC Chapel Hill Out of the Darkness Campus Walk brings students, faculty, and community members together to honor loved ones, support one another, and build connection around mental health and suicide prevention. It’s a meaningful opportunity for students to show up for their peers, raise awareness, and help create a more caring campus community. The walk is also a fundraising event, with proceeds supporting the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention’s work in education, advocacy, research, and programs for those affected by suicide.

Registration starts at 10:00 AM ET, with the walk starting at 11:00 AM ET. The walk will take place on UNC Chapel Hill Campus.

title: Sachets in the Historical Park
description: Plants and flowers have their own language and different combinations of them can be used to send messages in secret. Used for medicine, hygiene, and bug repellents during the American Revolution, come to the Historical Park and create your own sweet-smelling sachet. Our costumed interpreters will show you how. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Jones Lake Exploration Expo
description:
Join us for the 2nd annual Jones Lake Exploration Expo! It will be an exciting day of exploration and discovery at Jones Lake State Park! A variety of agencies and organizations will transform the park into a dynamic hub of interactive STEM activities and displays. Each table will feature a unique, hands-on experience designed to spark curiosity and engage minds of all ages. This family-friendly event promises fun, learning, and inspiration for kids and parents alike as you journey through our natural world.

This event is an offcial North Carolina Science Festival Event.

title: GGC Adventure Series: Lake Raleigh Paddle
description:
Back for another year of inspiring adventures in nature, this season of the Greenway Gear Collective Adventure Series will feature Adventure Race themed outings.
Each event will focus on one of the disciplines of adventure racing: traditionally mountain biking, orienteering (map and compass navigation), and paddling (canoe or kayak) and will take place at or around Lake Raleigh. Beginners or veterans alike will be in excellent hands with each adventure led by Debra and Jeff Rezeli, experts with over 11 years of adventure racing experience.
Register for each event here.

For those who love a goal to build towards...
The series will build skills in advance of a local practice 3-hour adventure race in May and the option to register and participate in a 5-hour adventure race hosted by Broad Run Offroad on June 13 & 14 at Lake Anna State Park in Spotsylvania, VA.
For more information on adventure racing, check out the US Adventure Racing Association website.

title: Hidden Battleship Tour
description:
Hidden Battleship is for the explorer at heart! Join us for an afternoon on a unique, behind-the-scenes tour of unrestored areas on the Battleship. Organized into small groups, the participants are led to areas in the bow, third deck, and below, and the superstructure, including all the way to the very top! This program requires climbing ladders, crossing high hatches, and going through tight/confined spaces. The tour is limited to ages 16 and older.

2026 Dates

► January 24
► February 28
► March 28
► April 18
► November 14

11 am – 5 pm; $75 (plus tax) per participant. This program always sells out, so get your Hidden Battleship ticket now! This program is for ages 16 and older.

This artisan market is more than just a collection of tents with cute gifts: It is a sweet celebration of local creativity featuring a variety of live music, food trucks, interactive demonstrations and local art and artisanal goods. Come out to browse, try your hand at the pottery wheel, have your tarot cards read and do plenty more.

title: The Student Art Sale
description:
Liven up your space or gift original art by supporting NC State's talented student artists at the Student Art Sale.

Since 2015, Arts NC State has hosted its annual art sale event, showcasing the talent and creativity of students.  The art sale welcomes submissions from all students, whether they study engineering, chemistry, media art design technology or any other field, making the event one of the nation's premier university art showcases. Every purchase at the art sale empowers students, fueling their artistic journey. Since its start, our art sale event has contributed over $100,000 to student artists, making a real impact on their lives and careers.

Join us in supporting and investing in the next generation of artists whose talents transcend disciplines.

The art sale will be held at the Gregg Museum of Art & Design.

The museum has limited parking. We strongly encourage guests to carpool or walk to the museum. Please view available parking options before your visit to the museum. ﻿

title: Wild About Wildflowers!
description:
April is the time of year when many of the wildflowers begin to bloom at Elk Knob State Park! Enjoy an NCSciFest event at Elk Knob by hiking with a Park Ranger on the Summit Trail to survey wildflowers. We will be using a couple of identification methods such as an ID guide and an app called iNaturalist. All observations will be photographed and uploaded to iNaturalist and NC State Park’s Natural Resource Inventory Database.

No survey and identification experience required to participate in this program! The Summit Trail is 1.9 miles one way and is moderate to strenuous. We will be meeting in the Summit Trail parking lot to begin. Dress appropriately to hike and bring water.

Please email marissa.mabe@ncparks.gov to register for this program.

title: Discover Tryon Palace: Outlander
location: Tryon Palace Historic Sites and Gardens
description:
Step into Tryon Palace and explore Diana Gabaldon’s award-winning Outlander series. Learn about the lives of the fictional Jamie and Claire Fraser, their family and friends, and interactions with historical figures and events in the tumultuous period of the American Revolution.

Tour covers storylines from Outlander books 4 – 8 and the Starz Outlander seasons 4 – 8. (For those not current on the books or show, there will be spoilers for these books and seasons.)

Tour begins at 301 George Street, across from the Tryon Palace Main Gates. Ticket holders should check in at the Waystation ticketing desk no later than 3:30 p.m.

title: MFA 2026 Thesis Exhibitions at Lump Gallery
description:
Department of Art and Art History

Lump Presents: UNC-Chapel Hill MFA Thesis Exhibitions

March 6th - March 29th:

INFRA ULTRA by CJ Murphy / Under the Thumb by Michelle Lisa Polissaint

Opening reception: March 6, 6-9 pm

April 3rd - April 26th:

Nepantla by Wendy Yamilett Moran / Growing Elsewhere: Ghorbatī (غربتی) by Rojano Mohammadzadeh / Blood, Sap, Dirt by Iris Moyan Wang

Opening reception: April 3, 6-9 pm

Lump Gallery is located at 505 S. Blount St., Raleigh, NC 27601

Opening Hours: Saturdays and Sundays, 1-5 pm, and by appointment.

Lump Gallery is pleased to present thesis exhibitions by UNC-Chapel Hill’s 2026 MFA graduating class.

At the conclusion of a rigorous, research-based two-year program, these artists consider the concept of place and their personal relationships to it. Each artist employs a range of media and responds to geographic, constructed, technological, and bodily autonomy. These relationships between place and personal narrative raise broader questions around positionality and society; communication and identity; and human impact. Revelation, purpose, and possibility guide each artist’s research-centered practices.

CJ Murphy creates works that render the intersection of technology, magic, and globalization material by reinvigorating found and discarded electrical waste. Michelle Lisa Polissaint draws on her experience in a strict religious community and deep research into the BITE method to discuss contemporary issues of indoctrination, high-control groups, and authoritarianism. Rojano Mohammadzadeh draws on Islamic prayer, talismanic imagery, text, and ritualized gestures to approach the immigrant experience through what she terms transplantation: survival through negotiating acceptance within a host body. Wendy Yamilett Morán’s practice explores the intersections of culture, realism, and spirituality through painting and ceramics, using their work to examine how identity, memory, and belonging are shaped within in-between spaces. Iris Moyan Wang is an interdisciplinary artist working primarily with ink painting and ceramics to create fluid and erotic hybridizations of bodies and botanicals that reimagine Chinese cultural heritage.

For press inquiries or other questions, please contact uncmfathesis@yahoo.com

title: BERT KREISCHER: PERMISSION TO PARTY Boxes4/18at7pm
Terrain Dance Project Concert: Themes of Gathering
description:
NC State’s Terrain Dance Project will present Themes of Gathering, a collection of new site-specific choreographic works at The Corner on Centennial Campus at 2 p.m. April 17 and 7 p.m. April 18. The Terrain Dance Project is a site-specific dance company dedicated to the creation and performance of choreographed and improvised dance works in nontraditional spaces, including dance film projects.

This interdisciplinary performance investigates The Corner as a site of gathering and identity, featuring live musicians and choreography by co-director Amy Love Beasley, guest artists Jill Guyton Nee and Anabelle Scarborough and NC State senior Annabelle Sharp. Select works explore the intersection of dance and science, inspired by work by NC State's Gage Lab and the North Carolina Sweetpotato Commission. This project is supported by an arts and science grant from the Office of Interdisciplinary Programs and Arts NC State.

In case of inclement weather on the scheduled performance dates, the performances will take place April 19 at 2 and 7 p.m.

description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: The Importance of Being Earnest by Oscar Wilde
description:
The Importance of Being Earnest, a Trivial Comedy for Serious People, is Oscar Wilde’s comedic play, which satirizes the conventions and manners of the late 19th century. Best friends Jack and Algernon each adopt fictional identities to escape social obligations. The plot unfolds with mistaken identities, romantic entanglements, clever wordplay and humorous social commentary.

The play's director is Danica Jenelle Jackson.

title: MFA 2026 Thesis Exhibitions at Lump Gallery
description:
Department of Art and Art History

Lump Presents: UNC-Chapel Hill MFA Thesis Exhibitions

March 6th - March 29th:

INFRA ULTRA by CJ Murphy / Under the Thumb by Michelle Lisa Polissaint

Opening reception: March 6, 6-9 pm

April 3rd - April 26th:

Nepantla by Wendy Yamilett Moran / Growing Elsewhere: Ghorbatī (غربتی) by Rojano Mohammadzadeh / Blood, Sap, Dirt by Iris Moyan Wang

Opening reception: April 3, 6-9 pm

Lump Gallery is located at 505 S. Blount St., Raleigh, NC 27601

Opening Hours: Saturdays and Sundays, 1-5 pm, and by appointment.

Lump Gallery is pleased to present thesis exhibitions by UNC-Chapel Hill’s 2026 MFA graduating class.

At the conclusion of a rigorous, research-based two-year program, these artists consider the concept of place and their personal relationships to it. Each artist employs a range of media and responds to geographic, constructed, technological, and bodily autonomy. These relationships between place and personal narrative raise broader questions around positionality and society; communication and identity; and human impact. Revelation, purpose, and possibility guide each artist’s research-centered practices.

CJ Murphy creates works that render the intersection of technology, magic, and globalization material by reinvigorating found and discarded electrical waste. Michelle Lisa Polissaint draws on her experience in a strict religious community and deep research into the BITE method to discuss contemporary issues of indoctrination, high-control groups, and authoritarianism. Rojano Mohammadzadeh draws on Islamic prayer, talismanic imagery, text, and ritualized gestures to approach the immigrant experience through what she terms transplantation: survival through negotiating acceptance within a host body. Wendy Yamilett Morán’s practice explores the intersections of culture, realism, and spirituality through painting and ceramics, using their work to examine how identity, memory, and belonging are shaped within in-between spaces. Iris Moyan Wang is an interdisciplinary artist working primarily with ink painting and ceramics to create fluid and erotic hybridizations of bodies and botanicals that reimagine Chinese cultural heritage.

For press inquiries or other questions, please contact uncmfathesis@yahoo.com

title: Story Walk- "The hike by: Alison Farrell
location: Carvers Creek State Park – Long Valley Farm Access
description: StoryWalk® is simply a book and a walking path combined.  In 2007, Anne Fergusson with the Vermont Department of Health had the idea to combine literacy and exercise. Teaming up with the Kellog Hubbard Library in Montpelier they attached laminated pages from a children’s book onto wooden posts.  In the ten years since, StoryWalks have gone up in all 50 states and at least 11 countries. The pages are affixed to metal sign stakes and a trail instantly becomes even more fun for children and book-lovers of all ages. StoryWalks® will usually remain up for one-to-two weeks along easy trails about one mile or less. The story featured this time is called "The Hike" by Alison Farrell. It will be along our Cypress Point Loop Trail.

title: Celebrating Our Colonial Roots - Online Plant Sale
location: Pick up 5/1/26 at Greenhouses behind Building N
description: The Brunswick County Master Gardeners Volunteer Association will hold an online plant sale starting on April 17, 2026 and running until April 24, 2026 with pick up day on May 1, 2026. This event celebrates the important colonial history of Brunswick County  through education on herbs, flowers, vines, shrubs and small trees which thrive here. Plants featured are those which our Colonial ancestors would have encountered along our coast and the Cape Fear River which are native to our region and support pollinators. Also featured will be plants from seeds which colonists brought with them from Europe and plants which recognize the contributions and life celebrations of the Gullah Geechee people who were enslaved in Africa to work the rice plantations along the Cape Fear River. This online plant sale celebrates the cultural traditions  found in gardening in our coastal region of the State.

title: Gettin’ daggity with it: Using DAGs as Tools for Variable Selection
description:
Ever pondered whether to include a variable in an analysis – is the variable necessary, will its inclusion/exclusion introduce bias in the analysis?  Why is the variable important anyways – is it a mediator, a moderator and a confounder some combination of these???

This course introduces how to incorporate DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs) in analysis planning to help think through what variables to collect and use (or refrain from using) in an analysis.

Topics include:

Introducing the structure of DAGs and the rules for using them to guide variable selectionA deep dive into the types of variable relations captured in DAGs with emphasis on mediation, moderation, effect modification, confounding and colliders and their potential to introduce bias in analysisHands on example of how to create and evaluate DAGs in R using the package daggityUse of DAGs may help inform data collection and analytical plans in ways that enhance reproducibility.

Course prerequisites: None. Knowledge of regression analysis and R is helpful but not required.

Registration and fee (refundable for UNCCH students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: The Art and Science of Plant Care
description:
Caring for plants is both an art and a science — part intuition, part know-how. Every gardener, from beginner to expert, faces the same essential questions: When should I prune? How much should I water? What’s the best way to plant, fertilize or repot? In this class, Bryce Lane will bring decades of horticultural expertise to demystify these everyday tasks, showing you not just what to do but why it works.

Over eight weeks, you’ll explore the fundamental practices that keep gardens thriving, learning how to perform each one with confidence and purpose. You’ll uncover the science behind good technique — understanding how soil, water, nutrients and plant biology interact — while also developing the practical judgment that comes only with experience.

Each session will focus on one key aspect of plant care, building a complete toolkit for growing healthier, more resilient plants in any garden setting.

title: Civic Hacking for Affordable Housing
description:
Code with the Carolinas - Triangle
Join volunteers at Code with the Carolinas as we help the [National Zoning Atlas](https://www.zoningatlas.org/) (NZA) team include North Carolina and South Carolina in their nationwide zoning atlas. "The National Zoning Atlas aims to depict key aspects of zoning codes in an online, user-friendly map." The Atlas aims to provide data for better solutions in transportation, environmental, and especially affordable housing policy.

Affordable housing proponents have long recognized zoning policies as barriers to affordable housing. To improve zoning policies, we first need to better understand the current state of zoning across the United States. Code with the Carolinas is helping this cause by joining the National Zoning Atlas to gather zoning data for North and South Carolina.

This is a multi-dimensional project grounded in data science with an end-goal of contributing to a nationally influential dataset. Our current work session activities focus on internet search and integrating data from multiple sources. The project as a whole has occasional opportunities for U/X and outreach and opportunities to learn about machine learning and natural language processing through the work of the NZA team. Volunteering on this project will provide excellent exposure to the foundations of civic data science, with a focus on GIS data.

Even if you're new to this project and/or civic tech volunteering, you're welcome to join the meeting. Visitors are always welcome and no technical skills are needed. You can learn more about the project on our [Slack Workspace](https://join.slack.com/t/codeforthecarolinas/shared_invite/zt-1kxuwu05x-3KxOpkOYjAuN5yuOAH8ROg) in the #project-zoning-atlas channel.

Meetings start with brief introductions. New volunteers are offered a personal orientation, often in a breakout room, while experienced volunteers get right to work. By the end of the session, both groups of volunteers are working together. We also contribute to the project through asynchronous work and informal work sessions.

Meetings are on the Jitsi platform and the link is provided before each meeting. If you will be joining on mobile, install the Jitsi app in advance [https://jitsi.org/downloads/](https://jitsi.org/downloads/) .

Learn more at [codewiththecarolinas.org](codeforthecarolinas.org) or reach out to info@codewiththecarolinas.org

All activities of Code with the Carolinas, a project of [Social Good Fund](https://www.socialgoodfund.org/), follow our [Code of Conduct](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/0d32d5f905f8fd49d5e80395f8fd47f2b59ce3ef8b335f72/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTkx) and are subject to this [Volunteer Waiver of Liability](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/06dd62200e6b28426b137e0e6b2848fb901487014f72cd16/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTE2).

title: Celebration of Sustainability
description:
Join NC State for a celebration of sustainability accomplishments on Tuesday, April 21, 2026. This annual event honors the people, projects and progress that advance sustainability at NC State.

The celebration will include a Sustainability Showcase starting at 8:30 a.m. where you can browse student displays on sustainability-related projects.  Students,  student organizations, departments and researchers are invited to share their sustainability-focused research and projects in a science-fair style format. Light refreshments will be served during this time.

Reserve your Sustainability Showcase Spot Here

A recognition program will start by 9:30 a.m. and include an announcement of NC State Sustainability Awards winners and NC State Sustainability Fund grant recipients. Following the program at 10:15 a.m., please stay for informal networking.

RSVP to attend the Celebration Here
This celebration is one of the hallmark events of NC State's Earth Month and is hosted by the NC State Sustainability Council, Climate and Sustainability Academy and the University Sustainability Office.

The campus community is invited to display their work and/or attend this event. Please RSVP by April 7:

Reserve your Sustainability Showcase Spot HereRSVP to attend the Celebration Here

Sponsored by the NC State University Sustainability Council, the Climate and Sustainability Academy and the University Sustainability Office, this event highlights the depth and impact of sustainability efforts across the university.

title: "Human-in-the-Loop” Thematic Analysis: Using R to Apply Current Recommendations for Incorporating LLMs into Qualitative Research
description:
Do you want to explore current research recommendations in the literature on incorporating LLMs into qualitative/mixed methods data analysis, or are you interested in implementing LLMs into your qualitative/mixed methods workflow?  If so, this short course is designed for you!

In this course we will examine current recommendations from the literature regarding integrating AI (specifically LLMs) into the process of qualitative coding.  Acknowledging that a wide range of qualitative approaches exist, all examples and recommendations in this course will be based on the 6 Phase Thematic Analysis Framework proposed by Braun & Clarke 2006 to simplify the course scope.

No perquisites in qualitative methods or coding are required for this course – just bring your computer & your curiosity!

In the course we will:

Review the qualitative approach of Thematic Analysis using the Braun & Clarke FrameworkGet a high-level, very big picture understanding of LLMs, what they are & how they generate “natural language”Examine current recommendations in the literature on incorporating LLMs in Thematic AnalysisDo hands-on activities in R that implement some of the current recommendations using the Braun & Clarke Framework. We will compare our results to published results generated by all human coders using the same dataRegistration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Climate and Sustainability EduJam
description:
The Climate and Sustainability Academy invites you to the Climate and Sustainability EduJam, a networking and tabling expo for anyone involved in climate and sustainability education at NC State. Whether you teach, research or create learning experiences, join us to connect with people and share ideas to strengthen climate and sustainability education at our university.

This is your chance to:

Showcase your courses, programs or curricular resources at a tableMeet peers and share ideas with others teaching or working in climate and sustainabilityDisplay a slide in our rotating slideshow, including QR codes or links so guests can follow up with youIf you would like to participate in Edujam, complete the interest form.

This event will immediately follow the Celebration of Sustainability event.

title: Overview of 2026 NIH Requirements: Biosketch and Current and Pending (Other) Support
description:
This session provides an overview of recent changes to the National Institutes of Health (NIH) grant application requirements, including new Common Forms that will be required for due dates on or after January 25, 2026.

In particular, this presentation will focus on the Biosketch and Current and Pending (Other) Support forms, which will need to be completed using the SciENcv tool. It will include demonstrations of SciENcv as well as ORCID iD creation and linking steps. The presentation aims to prepare researchers for the new NIH requirements by providing detailed instructions and resources to ensure compliance.

This program is offered by the University Libraries Health Sciences Library and led by Katherine Howell.

title: Kiana Bonollo: How I Built a Fashion Career Using Social Media
location: Wilson College of Textiles, W. Duke Kimbrell Atrium
description:
Kiana Bonollo '21, Content Creator and Patternmaker

Talk: How I Built a Fashion Career Using Social Media
From YouTube to Digital Products, Brand Partnerships & Entrepreneurship

What began as posting sewing projects on YouTube evolved into a multi-platform business built on digital sewing patterns, online education, and brand partnerships. In this talk, Kiana walks through how she identified a gap in the market, listened to her audience, and turned technical design skills into scalable digital products.

She will explore how social media has transformed entrepreneurship in the fashion industry: lowering marketing barriers, creating direct access to consumers, and opening new career paths beyond traditional corporate roles. Students will learn how to think strategically about social platforms, whether as influencers, entrepreneurs, or designers building their own brands.

The event is free but has limited capacity. Stay tuned for registration details!

title: *IN-PERSON* SF/F Group
description:
Durham Writers Group
We will be at the People's Solidarity Hub at 1809 Chapel Hill Rd, Durham. This venue does not serve food or drink, but feel free to bring your own. There is a kitchenette for our use, but be sure to clean up afterwards. The space might be a little cozy; please consider masking or staying home if you have a respiratory ailment.

This sub-group focuses on Speculative Fiction, including Science Fiction, Fantasy and Horror. If you have anything that falls under that category — be it short story, poem, novel-in-progress or screenplay — then come! We'd love to hear from you.

Our format allows each reader 15 minutes to present their work aloud, followed by approximately 20 minutes of feedback from the other attendees. To ensure fairness, each attendee is limited to 1 minute of Initial Feedback time. During that period, the author remains silent. Next, we have a 5 minute Free-For-All discussion about the work -- then the author may speak. (We usually get through three readers.)

Though not required, in order to receive more in-depth feed-back, readers may post their work in advance on our Google Group:
https://groups.google.com/d/forum/durham-sff-writers-group

We ask that participants keep their comments constructive and respectful, focusing on what works or doesn't work in the piece.

We also ask that readers provide a warning, both on the Google Group and during the meeting, if their story features content that some readers might find upsetting. This includes descriptions of violence or sexual content.

Readers are:
•
•
•

Backup Readers are:
•
•
•

Deep Backup:
•

***

***We meet every Tuesday at the same time, but alternate between In-Person*** ***and on Zoom. Members may attend both formats, if desired, but readers will be listed separately under the In-Person or Zoom weeks.***

title: Last Week of Spring Semester
description: Lab tests, semester project reports and presentations are permissible during the final week of classes. Papers and homework may have a due date during the final week of classes only if scheduled in the syllabus. However, in order for students to prepare for final examinations, faculty members may not assign other tests, quizzes or additional papers during the final week of classes. Assignments should not have a due date during the final exam period unless the assignment constitutes a part of the final exam. Exceptions to this policy must be approved in advance by the department head and the dean of the college involved. Students should contact the appropriate instructor or department head if they believe an assignment is not in compliance with this regulation.

title: Emerging Designers Textile Showcase
description:
The Emerging Designers Textile howcase highlights the work of senior textile design students in the fashion and textile design (FTD) program at the Wilson College of Textiles, celebrating the culmination of a semester-long capstone experience rooted in exploration, experimentation and refinement. Designers navigate the balance between creative freedom and creative discipline while leveraging access to state-of-the-art labs and studios. Collections span home textiles, apparel, fiber art and more.

The Emerging Designers Textile Showcase will be held at the Textiles Campus on Centennial Campus.

The Textile Showcase Opening is on Sunday, April 19, from 3 to 5 p.m. The event is open to the public and free for all.

The Textile Showcase will also be open:
- Monday, April 20, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m.
- Tuesday, April 21, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m.
- Wednesday, April 22, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m.

title: Story Walk- "The hike by: Alison Farrell
location: Carvers Creek State Park – Long Valley Farm Access
description: StoryWalk® is simply a book and a walking path combined.  In 2007, Anne Fergusson with the Vermont Department of Health had the idea to combine literacy and exercise. Teaming up with the Kellog Hubbard Library in Montpelier they attached laminated pages from a children’s book onto wooden posts.  In the ten years since, StoryWalks have gone up in all 50 states and at least 11 countries. The pages are affixed to metal sign stakes and a trail instantly becomes even more fun for children and book-lovers of all ages. StoryWalks® will usually remain up for one-to-two weeks along easy trails about one mile or less. The story featured this time is called "The Hike" by Alison Farrell. It will be along our Cypress Point Loop Trail.

title: Celebrating Our Colonial Roots - Online Plant Sale
location: Pick up 5/1/26 at Greenhouses behind Building N
description: The Brunswick County Master Gardeners Volunteer Association will hold an online plant sale starting on April 17, 2026 and running until April 24, 2026 with pick up day on May 1, 2026. This event celebrates the important colonial history of Brunswick County  through education on herbs, flowers, vines, shrubs and small trees which thrive here. Plants featured are those which our Colonial ancestors would have encountered along our coast and the Cape Fear River which are native to our region and support pollinators. Also featured will be plants from seeds which colonists brought with them from Europe and plants which recognize the contributions and life celebrations of the Gullah Geechee people who were enslaved in Africa to work the rice plantations along the Cape Fear River. This online plant sale celebrates the cultural traditions  found in gardening in our coastal region of the State.

Families, students and community members are welcome to attend and explore hands-on activities that connect farming, environmental technology and health.

A special highlight of this year’s event will be the N.C. A&T Innovation Bus, a state-of-the-art mobile learning space that will showcase cutting-edge technology, agriculture and innovation for youth and adults.

The Small Farm Unit is adjacent to the O'Berry Neuro-Medical Treatment Center, which is located at 400 Old Smithfield Road (NC-581) in Goldsboro.

title: Earth Day Craft

title: "Human-in-the-Loop” Thematic Analysis: Using R to Apply Current Recommendations for Incorporating LLMs into Qualitative Research
description:
Do you want to explore current research recommendations in the literature on incorporating LLMs into qualitative/mixed methods data analysis, or are you interested in implementing LLMs into your qualitative/mixed methods workflow?  If so, this short course is designed for you!

In this course we will examine current recommendations from the literature regarding integrating AI (specifically LLMs) into the process of qualitative coding.  Acknowledging that a wide range of qualitative approaches exist, all examples and recommendations in this course will be based on the 6 Phase Thematic Analysis Framework proposed by Braun & Clarke 2006 to simplify the course scope.

No perquisites in qualitative methods or coding are required for this course – just bring your computer & your curiosity!

In the course we will:

Review the qualitative approach of Thematic Analysis using the Braun & Clarke FrameworkGet a high-level, very big picture understanding of LLMs, what they are & how they generate “natural language”Examine current recommendations in the literature on incorporating LLMs in Thematic AnalysisDo hands-on activities in R that implement some of the current recommendations using the Braun & Clarke Framework. We will compare our results to published results generated by all human coders using the same dataRegistration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: Covidence: Simplify Literature Review Screening
description:
Learn how to streamline your systematic review process using Covidence, a collaborative tool for screening, data extraction, and quality assessment. This session is ideal for researchers planning or working on evidence synthesis projects like systematic or scoping reviews.

This workshop is offered by the UNC Health Sciences Library and led by Sydney Larson.

You will learn about the different plants and animals that can be found in some of the forests here in coastal North Carolina. After the hike you will get to help the ranger plant wildflower seeds in our new Earth Day Garden.

title: Legends & Landmarks: Paintings and Drawings by James C. Horton
description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Horton, which includes over 40 original paintings and drawings that highlight historic scenes and places from around the region and country that help tell America's story. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. Art is available for purchase and an Artist's Reception will be held on February 27th (6-8pm). Visit bellamymansion.org/exhibits for more information or email info@bellamymansion.org if you have questions.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Engineering with AI: Tools & Workflows for Next Gen Development Workshop
description:

You’ll leave with battle-tested frameworks, real experience, and a concrete plan to bring AI tools and automation into your daily dev process.

**[This is a ticketed event. Seats are limited! Learn more here!](https://ai.thisdot.co/workshops)**

title: Balancing Work and Life
description:
Using the philosophy that “those who are happy at home perform better on the job,” this workshop was developed for those who feel burdened with too many responsibilities and without enough time for personal enjoyment. This workshop will help participants explore and define home responsibilities, identify career requirements and prioritize leisure time. The resulting better balance will help achieve more in one’s career and at home.

Workshop Objectives
At the end of this workshop, you will be able to:
Identify the components of a balanced lifestyle
• Describe the concept of balance
• Identify what is out of balance in your life
• Describe how notions of balance have changed over time in your life and historically
• Identify the relationship between stress and balance
Identify and explore the balance between home responsibilities, career requirements
and leisure activities
• Describe how to create your own balance diagram
• Identify the right questions to achieve balance
• Describe the role of prioritization, limit setting and time management to balancing work and life
• Identify tips and suggestions on how to achieve balance
• Identify a personalized action plan to achieve balance in your life

Click on Carolina Talent registration link and select the “Request” button to automatically enroll.

title: Artful Introspection: Explore Identity, Creativity and Growth
description: Join Prevention Services for Artful Introspection, a mindful, creative series for exploring who you are and where you’re headed. Through crafting activities, such as comic strips and the Wheel of Life, you’ll build emotional awareness, connect with yourself and others and reflect on personal growth. Each 75-minute session will introduce a practical skill rooted in mindfulness, identity exploration and reflection. You’ll leave feeling more grounded, more connected and equipped with tools to support your growth. Crafting experience is not required.

title: Right to Be's Bystander Intervention to Stop Islamophobic and Xenophobic Harassment
description:
REGISTER HERE: https://righttobe.org/events/bystander-intervention-to-stop-islamophobic-and-xenophobic-harassment-13/

This 75-minute interactive training, brought to you by Right To Be, in partnership with the NYC Commission on Gender Equity, the Mayor’s Office of Equity and Racial Justice, and the NYC Commission on Gender Equity, will teach you Right To Be’s 5Ds of bystander intervention methodology. We’ll start by talking about the types of disrespect that Muslims and those who are perceived to be Muslim are facing right now — from microaggressions to violence — using a tool we call the “spectrum of disrespect.” You’ll learn what to look for and the positive impact that bystander intervention has on individuals and communities. We’ll talk through five strategies for intervention: distract, delegate, document, delay, and direct, and how to prioritize your own safety while intervening. We’ll have time at the end for practice, and you’ll leave feeling more confident intervening the next time you see Islamophobic and xenophobic harassment online or in person.

title: Game for Growth

All students are welcome to participate as often as they desire.

Join us for a meaningful afternoon of service and fun as we work together to care for our park. In honor of Earth Day, we’ll be picking up litter around the Paddy’s Creek Swim Beach and nearby picnic areas to help keep Lake James beautiful for everyone to enjoy.

After our cleanup, stick around for a round of “Oh, Deer,” an engaging and interactive game that explores wildlife survival.

We will meet at 3:30 PM in the Paddy’s Creek day-use area at the swim beach. All tools and personal protective equipment (PPE) will be provided. All ages are welcome to join.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980075/1:955dae8eb3e19e322904892b68496b6d7c43a8cf1d760785bd2e40b64adbad74/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage.

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country.

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare.

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

The program brings community members together monthly at the museum to discuss books, focusing on works by Black writers or featuring Black subjects, including fiction and non-fiction. Discussions will use questions to facilitate conversation.

description:
New Hope Bird Alliance is excited to welcome our community to participate in our New Hope Birdathon! Grab your binoculars, gather your friends, and get ready to bird this April 23-26 to see how many species you can find while raising funds for New Hope Bird Alliance. Our goal is to raise $15,000 to help expand our organization's capacity and hire our first paid staff, an Executive Director. This will enable us to amplify our educational and conservation efforts, bringing our passion for birds to a broader audience in the Triangle. The situation for birds and wildlife is deteriorating at an unsustainable pace, but together, we can make a difference. Your support will help us achieve our goals and ensure a brighter future for our feathered friends.

Birds need our help. Birds need new hope. Join the New Hope Birdathon and make your birding count. Learn more and register here.

title: Story Walk- "The hike by: Alison Farrell
location: Carvers Creek State Park – Long Valley Farm Access
description: StoryWalk® is simply a book and a walking path combined.  In 2007, Anne Fergusson with the Vermont Department of Health had the idea to combine literacy and exercise. Teaming up with the Kellog Hubbard Library in Montpelier they attached laminated pages from a children’s book onto wooden posts.  In the ten years since, StoryWalks have gone up in all 50 states and at least 11 countries. The pages are affixed to metal sign stakes and a trail instantly becomes even more fun for children and book-lovers of all ages. StoryWalks® will usually remain up for one-to-two weeks along easy trails about one mile or less. The story featured this time is called "The Hike" by Alison Farrell. It will be along our Cypress Point Loop Trail.

title: Celebrating Our Colonial Roots - Online Plant Sale
location: Pick up 5/1/26 at Greenhouses behind Building N
description: The Brunswick County Master Gardeners Volunteer Association will hold an online plant sale starting on April 17, 2026 and running until April 24, 2026 with pick up day on May 1, 2026. This event celebrates the important colonial history of Brunswick County  through education on herbs, flowers, vines, shrubs and small trees which thrive here. Plants featured are those which our Colonial ancestors would have encountered along our coast and the Cape Fear River which are native to our region and support pollinators. Also featured will be plants from seeds which colonists brought with them from Europe and plants which recognize the contributions and life celebrations of the Gullah Geechee people who were enslaved in Africa to work the rice plantations along the Cape Fear River. This online plant sale celebrates the cultural traditions  found in gardening in our coastal region of the State.

title: Staying Alive
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
March 14 – August 23, 2026

In the ultimate battle for survival, every animal has a plan.

Explore the striking ways nature fights back in “Staying Alive,” featuring 17 live animals from around the world with unbelievable self-defense strategies. Come face to face with trippy toads, armored armadillos, a two-toed sloth and more.

Discover how these defenders deploy an amazing arsenal of high-fashion camouflage and mimicry, specialized armor and advanced chemical warfare – like stink bombs, caboose cannons and smoke screens – to hide in plain sight, stand their ground or even drop a limb and make a run for it. Enjoy live animal encounters, larger-than-life sculptures, 3D tactile experiences and interactive programs.

Perfect for families and animal enthusiasts of all ages. Offered in English & Spanish.

title: Hybrid Lunchbox Talk: Hemiparasitic Plants in Grassland Restoration
description:
Full Title: Hybrid Lunchbox Talk: Bridging the Knowledge-to-Practice Gap: Hemiparasitic Plants in Grassland Restoration

With Jordan Argrett, PhD candidate, University of Georgia Odum School of Ecology

Thursday, April 23, 2026

12:00 PM-1:00 PM ET

Hybrid - Join us in person or on Zoom!

Free; preregistration required: https://reg.learningstream.com/reg/event_page.aspx...

Grasslands across North America, including the remnant prairies, glades, and savannas of the Southeast, are some of the most diverse and threatened ecosystems on Earth. Urban growth, habitat fragmentation, and changes in natural disturbances like fire and grazing have made it harder to restore native plant diversity at scale. In this Lunchbox Talk, learn about Jordan Argrett’s research on an underused restoration tool: native root hemiparasitic plants. These plants form symbiosis by tapping into the roots of their neighbors for nutrients. Acting as “pseudo-grazers,” they can make big impacts on plant communities by curbing dominant species, rebalancing the competitive landscape, and creating space for native wildflowers to thrive. Drawing on experimental ecology, conservation horticulture, and restoration strategies, Jordan’s work aims to provide southeastern conservation practitioners with practical, scalable tools to help bring back biodiversity.

ABOUT THE SPEAKER

Jordan Argrett is a 5th year PhD Candidate at the University of Georgia's Odum School of Ecology. Jordan is broadly interested in symbiosis and how species interactions can alter ecosystem processes. His dissertation research focuses on how plant-to-plant parasitism and plant-fungal mutualisms influence community structure and nutrient cycling in North American alpine meadows. Alongside his dissertation research, Jordan is a regional and local advocate for parasitic plants in the Southeast. By combining experimental ecology, restoration science, and community collaboration, Jordan aims to deliver actionable, evidence-based tools to conserve southeastern parasites, and utilize their unique ecology to enhance the success of grassland restoration efforts.
